name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Easy choice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Folks, I hate coming to you with bad news, so I’ll just say it: **we missed our mid-month goal yesterday.** These fundraising goals aren’t just numbers on a spreadsheet. Each dollar we raise goes directly into our organizing program – the work that persuades voters to turn out in November. **[_That’s why I’m asking you personally: can I count on you to be 1 of 5 people from your area to chip in $10 to help us close the gap in the next 5 hours? We’re so close!_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123475814/595578768/164382108?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.16_MissedGoal_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYzFiOWM3Ni0zN2YzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PML0iuIHyTDXIrc8kwAUJHrvVNF4LntckMQclHjgIHU=)** | | [ RUSH $10 >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123475816/595578770/164382108?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.16_MissedGoal_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYzFiOWM3Ni0zN2YzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PML0iuIHyTDXIrc8kwAUJHrvVNF4LntckMQclHjgIHU=)
---
Strong ground games win elections, full stop. Luckily, that makes the choice in front of us simple:
**We can invest in this organizing work now, turn out voters in November, and win.**
**Or we can look back after Election Day, wishing we had done more to stop Republicans from keeping the Senate majority.**
_I know what option I’m picking, and I hope you’ll join me._
**[_We still have one last chance to make up for lost ground today, but we have to move fast to get it done. Will you rush in $5, $10, or $25 to help us get back on track to hit our goals, build a strong organizing program, and flip the Senate?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123475818/595578772/164382108?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.16_MissedGoal_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYzFiOWM3Ni0zN2YzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PML0iuIHyTDXIrc8kwAUJHrvVNF4LntckMQclHjgIHU=)**

clean_body: Folks, I hate coming to you with bad news, so I’ll just say it: we missed our mid-month goal yesterday. These fundraising goals aren’t just numbers on a spreadsheet. Each dollar we raise goes directly into our organizing program – the work that persuades voters to turn out in November. = | | = Strong ground games win elections, full stop. Luckily, that makes the choice in front of us simple: We can invest in this organizing work now, turn out voters in November, and win. Or we can look back after Election Day, wishing we had done more to stop Republicans from keeping the Senate majority. I know what option I’m picking, and I hope you’ll join me. = | = | = | = | = | = With gratitude, Kirsten Gillibrand = * A message from DSCC Chair Kirsten Gillibrand: =

name: Chris Brooks, LCV
subject: URGENT: Trump orders dirty, outdated coal plants in Colorado, Indiana, Michigan, and Washington to stay open
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTM0MDkxMTIS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjM4MDE4MjY1NwS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
https://links.messages.lcv. We’re running out of time, Peter. Hundreds of people from around the country have stepped up today for Giving Tuesday, but we’re still running short of the 1,000 members we need to unlock an additional $100,000 for our work before midnight tonight. And with the Trump administration accelerating some of the most extreme anti-environment, pro-polluter attacks in modern history,falling short is simply not an option. We’re asking you, Peter: will you renew your LCV membership right now while membership is only $5 to help us unlock an additional $100,000 to oppose the Trump administration’s attempts to exploit our public lands and roll back climate progress? https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/G8bxcKP_RNB~ This isn’t just about hitting a number. It’s about having the resources we need to challenge these increasingly reckless orders and attempts to shred environmental safeguards — and stop the polluter handouts that Trump and his allies are fast-tracking. RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP >> https://links.messages.lcv. Every single person who steps up today is critical to our efforts — here’s why this Giving Tuesday is so important: Trump is trying to erase the scientific and legal foundation of climate action. By dismantling the Endangerment Finding, which affirms that greenhouse gas emissions harm our health and welfare, Trump’s EPA would hand fossil fuel companies a free pass to spew climate pollution into our air. He’s also ending the program that required big polluters to report their pollution, making it nearly impossible to track what they’re spewing or hold them accountable.
Decades of established pollution protections are being peeled away. More than a dozen rules that keep toxic pollution out of the air we breathe and water we drink to protect our health are on the chopping block, tossed aside to allow fossil fueled power plants to cut corners and shift the burden of their damage onto our communities.
The Trump administration is giving away the Arctic to corporate polluters, working to allow more drilling in one of the planet’s last intact ecosystems. Mining roads carved through national parks and preserves. Fire-sale auctions in critical ecosystems. Hundreds of millions of acres of U.S. waters off Alaska potentially opened to drilling. It could do irreparable damage to wildlife, our climate, and Alaska Native communities. We’re working every day with our 30 state affiliates, organizing on the ground, exposing backroom deals and corruption, rallying voters, and defending the treasured and unique places that define our country. But meeting this moment requires resources — especially as we go up against big polluters and fossil fuel opponents. This $100,000 match challenge, and how many people step up this Giving Tuesday, will determine how hard we can push back. Peter, your renewal right now powers our work to protect people and the planet. Will you renew before MIDNIGHT TONIGHT to help keep this movement strong when it matters most? $5 is all it takes. https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/~ Every contribution brings us closer. And the clock is ticking. Thanks for standing with us, Chris Brooks
Vice President of Membership
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=13409112&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjM4MDE4MjY1NwS2&spReportId=MjM4MDE4MjY1NwS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=13409112&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjM4MDE4MjY1NwS2&spReportId=MjM4MDE4MjY1NwS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/-

name: Alex Kelloff
subject: Good and bad news
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alex Kelloff for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak]I have some good news and bad news, friends.
I’ll give you the bad news first. A lot has happened over the past few weeks in Colorado. Between funding freezes for critical child care services, and political retaliation from the administration hurting our state, it’s been a challenging time for many hardworking CO families.
But the good news is, Coloradans are taking notice and they’re fed up. Recent polling shows Jeff Hurd is weaker than he wants anyone to believe, and I’m hearing the same thing on the campaign trail.
That’s why hitting every goal is going to be incredibly important from now until Election Day.
We set a goal to raise $6,500 by midnight to keep our coalition moving forward in this critical moment, and I’m counting on your support to meet our mid-month fundraising goal. Can you pitch in? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak?]
If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

name: Scott Wiener
subject: Update on the No Kings Act:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott WIENER FOR CONGRESS
body:
| |

**Peter - we have all been watching in horror as violence at the hands of Donald Trump’s secret ICE police force escalates across the country.** Last week in Minnesota, a masked ICE agent shot and killed Renee Good, a woman who posed no threat to him or others. And this Wednesday, ICE shot another man in the leg.

And yet under current law, __federal agents are virtually untouchable when they violate your rights.__

**We’re working to change that.**

My legislation, the No Kings Act, will give YOU the power to hold federal agents accountable when they use excessive force, illegally detain you, or trample your constitutional rights. **And just this week, it cleared a key committee.**

[**Add your name now to join our effort to hold federal agents accountable for their lawless actions →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123476355/595582413/-1099440156?refcode=SW_EM_EN_180DO_260116_2_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDY5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkN2RmYzMyNi0zOGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dcVvH4H1GJ7b7bAxT-kK8Ry4JAXEkVzwVYc9EiE7wnc=&emci=4947f029-2ff3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=d7dfc326-38f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=53530144)

The Trump Administration has made its position clear: they are standing behind the federal thugs who are brutalizing our communities.

The President and Secretary of Homeland Security are spreading dangerous lies to justify this violence. JD Vance has declared that the ICE officer who shot Renee Good has “absolute immunity.” And now Donald Trump is threatening to invoke the Insurrection Act to send the U.S. military into Minnesota to silence protestors.

**Let that sink in: an armed federal agent can gun down an innocent civilian, and the current administration will shield them from any and all accountability.**

This is Donald Trump’s extreme, fascist agenda at its worst. They want unaccountable, unidentifiable federal agents terrorizing immigrant families and everyday Americans alike.

**In a democracy, no one - not the President, not a federal agent, not anyone - should be above the law.**

We are not going to stand by while the Constitution is shredded and innocent people are gunned down by secret police. Together, we **MUST** keep fighting for accountability.

[**Sign on right now to join us in demanding that federal agents be held responsible for violating the law and terrorizing our communities.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123476360/595582420/-1099440156?refcode=SW_EM_EN_180DO_260116_2_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDY5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkN2RmYzMyNi0zOGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dcVvH4H1GJ7b7bAxT-kK8Ry4JAXEkVzwVYc9EiE7wnc=&emci=4947f029-2ff3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=d7dfc326-38f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=53530144)
| ****[SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123476366/595582425/-1099440156?refcode=SW_EM_EN_180DO_260116_2_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDY5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkN2RmYzMyNi0zOGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dcVvH4H1GJ7b7bAxT-kK8Ry4JAXEkVzwVYc9EiE7wnc=&emci=4947f029-2ff3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=d7dfc326-38f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=53530144)****

Thank you for joining us in this fight,

Scott Wiener

clean_body: | | Peter - we have all been watching in horror as violence at the hands of Donald Trump’s secret ICE police force escalates across the country. Last week in Minnesota, a masked ICE agent shot and killed Renee Good, a woman who posed no threat to him or others. And this Wednesday, ICE shot another man in the leg. And yet under current law, federal agents are virtually untouchable when they violate your rights. We’re working to change that. My legislation, the No Kings Act, will give YOU the power to hold federal agents accountable when they use excessive force, illegally detain you, or trample your constitutional rights. And just this week, it cleared a key committee. The Trump Administration has made its position clear: they are standing behind the federal thugs who are brutalizing our communities. The President and Secretary of Homeland Security are spreading dangerous lies to justify this violence. JD Vance has declared that the ICE officer who shot Renee Good has “absolute immunity.” And now Donald Trump is threatening to invoke the Insurrection Act to send the U.S. military into Minnesota to silence protestors. Let that sink in: an armed federal agent can gun down an innocent civilian, and the current administration will shield them from any and all accountability. This is Donald Trump’s extreme, fascist agenda at its worst. They want unaccountable, unidentifiable federal agents terrorizing immigrant families and everyday Americans alike. In a democracy, no one - not the President, not a federal agent, not anyone - should be above the law. We are not going to stand by while the Constitution is shredded and innocent people are gunned down by secret police. Together, we MUST keep fighting for accountability. | ** Thank you for joining us in this fight, Scott Wiener | | | Scott Wiener is running for Congress to fight for San Francisco \- to defend our democracy, build affordable housing, expand healthcare, tackle the climate crisis, and make government work for people again. Join our campaign to help deliver bold, practical progress for California and the country. You can learn more about Scott Wiener on his , make a =, or sign up to . name: Katie Porter
subject: How we demand justice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
Donald Trump has spent months sowing chaos and trying to turn our cities into war zones.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40718%2E3422442%2EwwV8Ls)
Peter,
What ICE is doing is putting all of us in danger.
Donald Trump has spent months sowing chaos and trying to turn our cities into war zones.
From California to Minnesota, he has deployed untrained, masked, and armed ICE agents to run amok in our neighborhoods and terrorize our communities.
**_There must be accountability._**
We must be very clear about California's unwillingness to ever cooperate with unconstitutional, immoral, and unlawful activity. **That is what ICE is engaged in, and it has no place in the Golden State.**
We can’t afford to wait until an election day to raise our voices. Our fight for justice is happening right now. We have the power to stand up and speak out, but only if we do it together.
**[Peter, will you sign our petition demanding accountability for ICE agents who break the law?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21696?c0=em_ms_kp_20260116_1_ak.C9ZRBP&source=em20260116-40718&t=3&akid=40718%2E3422442%2EwwV8Ls)**

name: Gina Hinojosa
subject: Families across Texas are living in fear
body:
We can demand border security and protect our humanity.
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
Peter,
I grew up on the border in a mixed-status family, like many Latinos in Texas.
In the Rio Grande Valley, we’re proud of who we are. Proud of our culture, proud of our families and the community we’ve built, and proud to be American. **So when our identity is questioned and our neighbors are treated like suspects, we feel threatened.**
Right now, families across Texas are terrified. Longtime members of our communities are being taken in ICE raids. Parents are scared to go to work. Kids are scared to go to school. And masked agents with no accountability are showing up in our neighborhoods — using force without transparency.
_This isn’t border security._
When Texans asked for border security, they didn’t mean masked men violently ripping neighbors out of their communities. They didn’t mean disorder and chaos.
I believe we can demand border security and keep Texans safe without dehumanizing our neighbors. That’s the kind of leadership I want to bring to the Governor’s office.
We need to show that Texans are ready for something better. **[If you’re with me, can you chip in $5 today to help us keep building this campaign?](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260116_2_ice.T8YCj_&refcode=em20260116-421&amount=5&t=1&akid=421%2E299639%2EtALM4y)**
[Donate $5 »](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260116_2_ice.T8YCj_&amount=5&refcode=em20260116-421&t=20&akid=421%2E299639%2EtALM4y)
Thank you,
Gina

[ Donate » ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_gh_footer?refcode=em20260116-2&t=21&refcode2=421_299639_tALM4y&akid=421%2E299639%2EtALM4y)

name: DNC HQ
subject: Jerome Powell:
body:
---
Derek, **FIRST:** Trump and his allies tanked the economy, skyrocketing prices and making jobs harder and harder to find. **THEN:** The Trump administration attempted to deflect the blame, announcing a politically motivated investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. They’re once again weaponizing the justice system for their own political gain. **NOW:** It’s more important than ever that we elect a Democratic Congress to bring costs down, stop more authoritarian power grabs, and reel in the madness. In the last few days, thousands of people have chipped in to support our work to elect Democrats up and down the ballot. Now, they’re counting on you to join them. [**None of this is normal, so, Derek, we're asking: Can we count on you to pitch in $7 or whatever makes sense for you — directly to the DNC today and help strengthen our ability to fight for our democracy in every corner of the country?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?refcode=em_20260116_5809_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5809_22930333_54NZ89&akid=5809%2E22930333%2E54NZ89) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260116_5809_fr&refcode2=5809_22930333_54NZ89&akid=5809%2E22930333%2E54NZ89)**

name: Kamala Harris
subject: A wild idea
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for the People PAC ([fightforthepeoplepac
body:
Make a contribution of ANY AMOUNT today, and I will send you a copy of 107 Days

**I want to try something a bit different.**
This is not an email I am sending to everyone, but it is something I want to get in front of people who have supported me in the past.
I am so thrilled to be back on the road to talk about my book, _107 Days_ , and the fight for our future.
Writing and talking about the 107 days I spent running for president was like living the campaign in reverse. It meant revisiting every triumph and heartbreak, and it meant an honest assessment of what we experienced, what we learned, where we fell short, and how we tackle the fight ahead with determination and optimism.
At the same time, we have a lot of important work to do for the 2026 elections that requires our immediate resources and attention. I want to do everything in my power to support leaders across our nation who will fight for our future.
So here’s the part that’s a bit different...
If you make a contribution to Fight for the People PAC of ANY AMOUNT, I'll send you a copy of _107 Days_. The average cost per book is $22.50 including shipping. If you can give that, great. If you can only give $5, that is just fine. If you want to give more to cover the cost for others who might not be able to afford to give as much, that would be fantastic.
[**So please: Make a contribution of ANY AMOUNT today, and I will send you a copy of _107 Days_. I am so thrilled to be back on the road to talk about my book, 107 Days , and the fight for our future. Writing and talking about the 107 days I spent running for president was like living the campaign in reverse. It meant revisiting every triumph and heartbreak, and it meant an honest assessment of what we experienced, what we learned, where we fell short, and how we tackle the fight ahead with determination and optimism. name: Texas Organizing Project PAC
subject: Can you believe this!?
disclaimer_text: paid for by Texas Organizing Project PAC, organizetexas
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560]
Peter,
Our state MAGA officials CONTINUE to sell Texans out.
Last week, the Texas Secretary of State’s office turned over the state’s voter roll to the U.S. Justice Dept. [https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/09/texas-voter-roll-trump-administration-justice-department-democrats-dnc/] to comply with the Trump admin’s demands for access to data on millions of voters across the country.
You read that right. The man who for years has spread the lie that he lost the 2020 presidential election because of “widespread voter fraud” is now gaining unprecedented access to voter registration data, state by state.
Of course, Texas Republicans put up no resistance to this and just play along with the president’s increasingly-authoritarian policies. These Republicans need an electoral reality check, Peter.
SUPPORT TOP PAC’s work to get out the vote and counter MAGA in 2026! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560]
We know news like this is ridiculous and upsetting. But remember, we aren't helpless. Collectively, we have the power to fight back. And we can channel that power through our votes.
PITCH IN to provide us with the resources we need to reach more Texas voters in the midterms! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560]
Your contribution – no matter the amount – puts fuel in our tank to help shift the political landscape of our state. DONATE $15, $25, $50 or any amount you can. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=VoterRolls&amount=15.00&refcode2=00f5d1f9-41f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560]
We appreciate you!
– Texas Organizing Project PAC

body:
Peter,
[I'm not going to sugarcoat it. **Here's the truth:** When you're in combat, you don't get do-overs. When you're running a business, you don't get extensions. But in a campaign, sometimes you have to make the tough call to keep fighting. That's what I'm doing right now. **Extended Deadline:** Tonight at 11:59 PM
**What We Still Need:** __$31,000__
**Why It Matters:** This determines if we can compete statewide I've been outspent before. I've been counted out before. But I've never quit, and I'm not starting now. Michigan needs a governor who knows what it's like to meet a payroll. Who understands the sacrifices our law enforcement makes. Who will fight for parents, not bureaucrats. **I can be that governor. But only with your help.** If you didn't get a chance to contribute yesterday, this is it. If you've been waiting to get involved, this is the moment. clean_body: | | | Friend, I'm not going to hide behind spin or political talk. We missed our deadline last night. And I'm extending it for 24 hours because Michigan's future is too important not to. Here's the truth: When you're in combat, you don't get do-overs. When you're running a business, you don't get extensions. But in a campaign, sometimes you have to make the tough call to keep fighting. That's what I'm doing right now. Extended Deadline: Tonight at 11:59 PM What We Still Need: $31,000 Why It Matters: This determines if we can compete statewide I've been outspent before. I've been counted out before. But I've never quit, and I'm not starting now. Michigan needs a governor who knows what it's like to meet a payroll. Who understands the sacrifices our law enforcement makes. Who will fight for parents, not bureaucrats. I can be that governor. But only with your help. If you didn't get a chance to contribute yesterday, this is it. If you've been waiting to get involved, this is the moment. After tonight, there are no more chances. We move forward with what we have. Let's make sure we have what it takes to win. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Thank you, teachers
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek —

I was moving some boxes of old stuff into the attic recently and found a
couple of scrapbooks. One of them included a photo from high school that I
just had to share with you.

This photo is of me after winning a competition for a speech on America’s
free enterprise system, believe it or not. (Leave it to a high school
student whose only relevant experience was working in a restaurant and his
father’s lumber yard to opine on the economy.) The envelope contained a
check for first prize — it was a hundred dollars. That was a lot of money
back then. It still is. Just take a look:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q12026_abs_em.43HrbP&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]Adam after winning a competition for a speech on America’s free
enterprise system

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=q12026_abs_em.khhTGf&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1005&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]Before I go on, and I realize this is a different type of email than
you’re getting from most campaigns right now, can you pitch in $10 to help
fuel my fight to protect our democracy and flip the House and Senate?

When I was 11 years old, my dad moved our family from Scottsdale, Arizona,
to the San Francisco Bay Area. I started in a new middle school before
attending Monte Vista High School in Danville.

I played on the soccer team and had several wonderful teachers who really
made a mark on me. There was Mr. Morrison, who taught contemporary world
problems; Ms. Shackleford, my calculus teacher; and the other Mr.
Morrison, who taught physiology and had us dissecting fetal pigs — I can
still smell the formaldehyde. Ugh.

But the two teachers who left the biggest mark on me were Ms. Abbott, my
Shakespeare teacher, and Al Gentile, my speech coach.

Ms. Abbott took our class up to Ashland, Oregon, each summer for the
Shakespeare festival, but it was Mr. Gentile who taught us the art of
public speaking. That was the after-school activity that really got me
going, and I was soon competing in speech tournaments. At first, I wasn’t
very good, scoring 5s at tournaments, which was, well, the lowest score
you could get. I did dramatic interpretation — Twelve Angry Men and
Inherit the Wind were a couple of favorites — I loved those plays, but ...
they didn’t love me.

Then I switched to Original Oratory, and things started to change. In
“OO,” you could write your own material, craft a speech on a topic of your
own choice, and that made all the difference. Hence, the picture before
you now.

Although speaking before others did not come naturally to me, I worked
hard at it, reading great speeches from Lincoln and Frederick Douglass,
and listening to recordings of Robert Kennedy, Martin Luther King, Jr.,
Winston Churchill, John F. Kennedy, and more.

I couldn’t get enough. From the tinny phonograph in my bedroom came these
wonderful, if scratchy, recordings of the greatest speakers of the modern
era. It was mesmerizing. And from my brother’s room next door, the sounds
of Richard Burton, Laurence Olivier, and other greats performing
Shakespeare.

To this day, I draw on those voices and those speeches for inspiration
when I’m writing a speech. For my core thesis, I gather the elements that
I want to weave together from history, the law or Constitution, or, in the
case of an investigation, the key pieces of evidence.

Perhaps most importantly, I try to outline the speech I want to give in my
head, without the tyranny of a blank page in front of me. Only when I have
a good idea of how I want to structure the piece do I sit down to write.
Ideally, in a comfy chair by the fire.

During the first impeachment trial of President Trump, I would take notes
on the key elements that I thought should be incorporated into a closing
argument for the day. If I didn’t have time to write the closing, I would
put numbers next to each of those bullets to put them in some logical
order that told a story. And then I would improvise.

But occasionally, I had a whole evening to write a closing argument, and
there, I waited until my thoughts coalesced around the theme and its most
logical order before putting pen to paper, or more accurately, fingers to
iPad!

Now that I’m in the Senate, and Trump is back in office, I am once again
drawing on history, the law, and the Constitution to respond to the
actions of this administration from the Senate floor or speaking to
constituents in town halls.

Some things never change. But the lessons from those early years — and
from those teachers — have stayed with me:

The need to speak plainly, but not dumb down your words. The need to
communicate in a way that draws on the inherent beauty of our language and
our higher aspirations and ideals. And the need to appeal to the best in
human nature, not the worst. There is unlimited power in words — good and
bad — and I never forget that.

I hope these lessons have helped me be a more effective advocate for our
democracy. My parents and teachers taught me that certain very basic
values really mattered. That truth matters, that decency matters, and that
our democracy matters. And it’s up to each one of us to use the skills we
have to fight for them.

And it’s why I’m asking you today:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=q12026_abs_em.khhTGf&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1006&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]Will you chip in $10 to our campaign to protect our democracy today? We
can flip the House and Senate in this election, and we need your support.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1040?c0=q12026_abs_em.Tpzh5t&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1007&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]EXPRESS DONATE $5

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1015?c0=q12026_abs_em.U9tr8L&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1008&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]EXPRESS DONATE $10

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1016?c0=q12026_abs_em.Pm2-3I&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1009&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]EXPRESS DONATE $25

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1017?c0=q12026_abs_em.hNFk0m&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1010&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]EXPRESS DONATE $50

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1018?c0=q12026_abs_em.U1Qhx6&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1011&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]EXPRESS DONATE $100

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2142?c0=q12026_abs_em.m3iFAN&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&t=1012&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]ANOTHER AMOUNT

It’s up to each of us to fight for what we believe in and for our
democracy, however best we can. And I want to thank you for being a part
of that fight.

Thanks,

Adam

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q12026_abs_em.43HrbP&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1013&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]Adam Schiff for Senate Banner; text Schiff to 724433

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2342?c0=q12026_abs_em.QtEv2H&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260116_PM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=1014&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]DONATE MONTHLY

135 E. Olive Ave., #750 Burbank CA, 91502

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/60?t=1002&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]www.adamschiff.com | [ mailto:contact@adamschiff.com ]contact@adamschiff.com | [ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/64?t=1003&akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA ]Privacy Policy

PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.adamschiff.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=9419%2E5257348%2EqBloBA

subject: Rooting out corruption by following the money
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123478220/595626125/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODI4M2MxMi00OGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=90_a3DQE7bjgSP_n00HkqHYTy6S6FVfhCxZhFqvKCpw=&emci=d669f920-36f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=08283c12-48f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=9818364)

| | For over three years now, my investigative team and I have been conducting a “follow the money” investigation into Jeffrey Epstein and his co-conspirators. Our investigation first started by digging into the financial dealings of billionaires and wealthy financiers with known ties to Epstein. As we continued to follow the money, my investigators unraveled more and more details about Epstein’s crimes. From the jump, we’ve known that there must have been big money behind Epstein’s international sex-trafficking operation. He had countless wealthy individuals, banks, and companies helping him get away with – and financing – his crimes. Last year, my investigators unveiled bombshell findings showing that the Treasury Department is sitting on a vast trove of financial documents showing how Epstein made his money, and who helped enable his criminal operation. These documents contain an ironclad paper trail. They show who helped finance Epstein’s activities, his clients, and what girls and women he paid off. My investigators have seen records detailing many of these transactions with their own eyes. Predictably, through all of this, the Trump administration continues to stonewall our investigation by refusing to release the files sitting at the Treasury Department. Virtually none of the essential information has been released, and I continue to keep digging to make sure justice is served. That’s why access to the Treasury documents and following the money is so essential. That investigation can’t be tampered with. It provides a clear paper trail between Epstein and his co-conspirators. In a case as big as this one, facts and evidence matter. clean_body: | | | | For over three years now, my investigative team and I have been conducting a “follow the money” investigation into Jeffrey Epstein and his co-conspirators. Our investigation first started by digging into the financial dealings of billionaires and wealthy financiers with known ties to Epstein. As we continued to follow the money, my investigators unraveled more and more details about Epstein’s crimes. From the jump, we’ve known that there must have been big money behind Epstein’s international sex-trafficking operation. He had countless wealthy individuals, banks, and companies helping him get away with – and financing – his crimes. Last year, my investigators unveiled bombshell findings showing that the Treasury Department is sitting on a vast trove of financial documents showing how Epstein made his money, and who helped enable his criminal operation. These documents contain an ironclad paper trail. They show who helped finance Epstein’s activities, his clients, and what girls and women he paid off. My investigators have seen records detailing many of these transactions with their own eyes. Predictably, through all of this, the Trump administration continues to stonewall our investigation by refusing to release the files sitting at the Treasury Department. Virtually none of the essential information has been released, and I continue to keep digging to make sure justice is served. That’s why access to the Treasury documents and following the money is so essential. That investigation can’t be tampered with. It provides a clear paper trail between Epstein and his co-conspirators. In a case as big as this one, facts and evidence matter. Historically speaking, the best way in America to root out corruption is to follow the money wherever it leads. I’m in the home stretch of our investigation, building the strongest case possible to help make sure charges stick against those who were closest to Epstein. We are not going to let up until justice is served, the facts come out, and reforms are implemented to make sure this can never happen. name: Scott Peters
subject: Rejecting reckless cuts

body:

| | Peter – From dangerous culture wars to endless obstruction, we’ve seen what happens when extremists drive the agenda: progress stalls, communities are ignored, and families pay the price.

That’s why I’m fighting for funding packages that actually deliver for San Diego, and why I helped pass a bipartisan bill that rejects the Administration’s reckless domestic cuts and invests in priorities our community depends on.

This package includes **$158 million to address the cross-border sewage crisis** , strengthens election security, supports small businesses and entrepreneurs, and protects investments in low-income communities across San Diego. It also pushes back against attempts to gut programs that help families and local job creators succeed.

This is what responsible governing looks like. But national groups and outside interests don’t like it, and they’re already gearing up to attack Democrats who are willing to stand up and get things done.

clean_body: | | Peter – From dangerous culture wars to endless obstruction, we’ve seen what happens when extremists drive the agenda: progress stalls, communities are ignored, and families pay the price. That’s why I’m fighting for funding packages that actually deliver for San Diego, and why I helped pass a bipartisan bill that rejects the Administration’s reckless domestic cuts and invests in priorities our community depends on. This package includes $158 million to address the cross-border sewage crisis , strengthens election security, supports small businesses and entrepreneurs, and protects investments in low-income communities across San Diego. It also pushes back against attempts to gut programs that help families and local job creators succeed. This is what responsible governing looks like. But national groups and outside interests don’t like it, and they’re already gearing up to attack Democrats who are willing to stand up and get things done. With continued grassroots support from people like you, we can make sure leaders who fight for San Diego have the resources to stand strong, push back against reckless agendas, and keep delivering results. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = I’m proud of the work we’re doing – and I’m grateful to have you with me in this fight. Scott | | | = | As a leader among Democrats in Congress, Scott Peters has one of the strongest track records of achieving progressive reforms and getting things done. He’s committed to protecting our environment, improving access to affordable healthcare, implementing compassionate immigration reforms and protecting reproductive freedom. Democrats in swing districts like Scott’s rely on grassroots supporters like you.**= | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Scott Peters for Congress Scott Peters for Congress PO Box 22074 San Diego, CA 92192 United States

name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. | Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23826%2E4449941%2EY14tsk) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23826%2E4449941%2EY14tsk) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/72085?t=3&akid=23826%2E4449941%2EY14tsk) Hey team, it’s Mark Kelly.

Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.

I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.

But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.

So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.

I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.

We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.

After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.

[**I’m asking you to split a donation between Fight For Reform and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/72085?t=4&akid=23826%2E4449941%2EY14tsk) | | [DONATE $5 NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/72086?amount=5&amounts=25%2C50%2C100%2C250&t=5&akid=23826%2E4449941%2EY14tsk)

Thank you.

Mark

name: MGP
subject: A quick note
body:
| | Peter – I wanted to send one more quick note to close out the week.

I know people are tired of the noise and budgets are tight, and I don’t take asking for support lightly. But I also know what’s at stake.

This seat only stays independent if we fight for it, together. Outside groups are already trying to decide our future for us, and I’m not going to let that happen without pushing back.

[If you’re able, please chip in $10 right now to help me keep doing the work I was elected to do.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123478524/595627181/-1425034552?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_260116_2_5&refcode2=9c514e8a-4af3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YzUxNGU4YS00YWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9wvqXQoH3M18P8qAXPdOC6MW_BHMEZko34cmaxKwiEM=) _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._ | | **[DONATE $10 NOW »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123478525/595627182/-1425034552?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_260116_2_5&refcode2=9c514e8a-4af3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YzUxNGU4YS00YWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9wvqXQoH3M18P8qAXPdOC6MW_BHMEZko34cmaxKwiEM=)**

name: Tim Walz
subject: Trump’s justice department is investigating me
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
#
Peter,
Two days ago it was Elissa Slotkin. Last week it was Jerome Powell. Before that, Mark Kelly.
Now, the federal government has opened an investigation into me.
This comes after a federal ICE agent fatally shot Renee Good in Minneapolis. But instead of focusing on accountability for that tragic shooting, federal authorities are turning their attention toward a governor who demanded transparency and answers.
The only person not being investigated for the shooting of Renee Good is the federal agent who shot her.
Weaponizing the justice system and threatening political opponents is a dangerous, authoritarian tactic. It undermines public trust and distracts from the real issue: a Minnesotan lost her life, and justice has yet to be served.
I won’t back down from defending Minnesotans or standing up for the rule of law. But these fights are expensive by design — meant to intimidate and drain resources so people who speak up are forced to stop.
**[If you’re able, please contribute $5 to our Legal Defense Fund today and help ensure we can keep fighting for accountability, transparency, and justice.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/235?refcode=tw-em-20260116-419&amount=5&t=1&akid=419%2E34582%2EGXr9m4)**
Thank you for standing with me in this moment.
I’m grateful,
Tim Walz

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dgalegalfund?refcode=tw-em-20260116-419&t=2&refcode2=419_34582_GXr9m4&akid=419%2E34582%2EGXr9m4)**
---
Email us: [info@walzforgovernor.org](mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=419%2E34582%2EGXr9m4)

Prepared and paid for by Walz for Governor
Tim Walz for Governor
P.O. Box 4337
St. Paul MN 55104

clean_body: The GOP is moving forward ... FAST. | | | Friend, This isn’t public. It was hand-delivered to those who’ve stood by our movement when it mattered most. Just a strategic move that reveals what’s at stake and who they trust to carry the banner in the Senate. This is the Senate race that will define the America First future. This may be the only chance you’ll get to see what these GOP leaders just decided — before it breaks wide open. Read it now. Peter,I’ve spent the last few weeks in D.C. and back home looking for one thing: **a bit of good news.** But after talking to our top leaders, the truth is __much tougher__ than I expected. I just sat down to write a **raw** , **honest** note about exactly where we stand and why the 2026 cycle has me more concerned than ever before. I’m being as vulnerable as I can be because the stakes for our country have never been higher. I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing _everything_ they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?

clean_body: | | | Peter,I’ve spent the last few weeks in D.C. and back home looking for one thing: a bit of good news. But after talking to our top leaders, the truth is much tougher than I expected. I just sat down to write a raw , honest note about exactly where we stand and why the 2026 cycle has me more concerned than ever before. I’m being as vulnerable as I can be because the stakes for our country have never been higher. I can’t stand in this gap alone.With a determined spirit,Katie Britt | | I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong. Faith. Family. Freedom. The Left is doing everything they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored. From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul. This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere. Will you stand with me? Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc. |

name: Tom Fitton
subject: VICTORY: Historic Supreme Court Election Law Ruling
body:
Judicial Watch Sues to Reveal Biden Cover-Up on Iran Nuclear Program

[INSIDE JW]

THE MOST IMPORTANT SUPREME COURT ELECTION LAW RULING IN A GENERATION

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36872&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9fcW1sNWxQMDJDbz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=9ad73a58c4c8b0df24a31078c11b7ac5e84f26a031999165858f1f79b8917e1c]

This week, the U.S. Supreme Court issued a sweeping 7-2 decision
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36855&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2Jvc3Qtdi1pbGxpbm9pcy1zY290dXMtcnVsaW5nLTIwMjYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=a9d742bb1853c0e6e654cf5de218996afb805c3987e30fa32cf954e145719094]
in our historic election case, Rep. Michal J. Bost et. al. v. Illinois
State Board of Elections, broadly affirming candidate standing to
bring election disputes to federal court.

As you know from my prior updates, we filed this lawsuit on behalf of
Congressman Mike Bost and two presidential electors who were seeking
to vindicate their standing to challenge an Illinois law which allows
the counting of ballots received up to 14 days after Election Day.

This is the most important Supreme Court election law ruling in a
generation. Too many courts have denied candidates the standing to
challenge unlawful election rules such as the outrageous accepting of
ballots that arrive after Election Day. American citizens concerned
about election integrity should celebrate this victory. I thank our
legion of supporters and our election law team that helped achieve
this historic result.

The Supreme Court decision broadly affirmed candidate standing to
bring election ballot disputes to federal court:

> Candidates have a concrete and particularized interest in the rules
> that govern the counting of votes in their elections, regardless
> whether those rules harm their electoral prospects or increase the
> cost of their campaigns. Their interest extends to the integrity of
> the election — and the democratic process by which they earn or
> lose the support of the people they seek to represent.

“I’m thankful the U.S. Supreme Court has ruled strongly in our
favor and concluded we have standing to challenge Illinois’
unconstitutional law allowing vote-by-mail ballots to be counted two
weeks after Election Day,” said Congressman Bost. This is a
critically important step forward in the fight for election integrity
and fair elections. I look forward to continuing to pursue this case
as we navigate the next stages of the legal process. It’s vitally
important that we restore the people’s trust in our elections.”

We originally filed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=27407&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdC1hZ2FpbnN0LWlsbGlub2lzLXRvLXN0b3AtY291bnRpbmctb2YtYmFsbG90cy1hZnRlci1lbGVjdGlvbi1kYXkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=1b8b2e7ef9a18e3f797a44d49d3fad7b007089973d519a020f4f559472dd9712]
this on May 25, 2022, in the U.S. District Court for the Northern
District of Illinois. The lower court dismissed the claim for lack of
standing, and the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit upheld
the lower court ruling in August 2023 (_Rep. Michael J. Bost, Laura
Pollastrini, and Susan Sweeney v. The Illinois State Board of
Elections and Bernadette Matthews_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=28345&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL21pY2hhZWwtYm9zdC1ldC1hbC12LWlsLWJvYXJkLW9mLWVsZWN0aW9ucy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDI3NTQvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f4376e8898ff95bf9e3947157b9ae5b6c5656a891d5567d3dadc20895ed43f3c
_(No.
1:22-cv-02754, 23-2644, 24-568)).

We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As
part of our work, we assembled a team of highly experienced voting
rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii and
cleaned up voter rolls across the country, among other achievements
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=35695&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL2VsZWN0aW9uK2xhdy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=9397a9e3d745abe0c3f21cda7e7ee80355607c49f190c9e5632b403f671aff4b].

Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election
law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil
Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting
rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements
in dozens of states.

T. Russell Nobile, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, is part of our
voting integrity efforts and focuses on campaign and voting issues,
civil rights issues, constitutional law, official misconduct by public
institutions and officials, and other issues.

Eric Lee is an attorney at Judicial Watch, where he focuses on
enforcing federal and state laws that promote transparency and
integrity in the electoral process. Eric graduated with his B.A. from
St. Mary’s College of Maryland and received his J.D. from the
University of Maryland School of Law. He is licensed to practice in
California, Maryland, the District of Columbia, and in federal courts
in Illinois and Colorado.

Paul Clement, who has argued more than 100 cases before the Supreme
Court, represented Congressman Bost and the electors with Judicial
Watch before the Supreme Court. Clement is former solicitor general
under President George W. Bush from 2005-2008 and is widely regarded
as among the top Supreme Court litigators in the country.

In November 2025, the Supreme Court granted review
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36458&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc3VwcmVtZS1jb3VydC1taXNzaXNzaXBwaS1iYWxsb3QtZGVhZGxpbmUtY2FzZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=afaedc6d451eed9a2fc08750f7a924bc9e68deb2c0eb045724f302a83613ebe2]
in a landmark election integrity case brought on behalf of the
Libertarian Party of Mississippi. The case seeks to uphold a ruling by
the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit, which struck down a
Mississippi law unconstitutionally allowing election officials to
count mail-in ballots received up to five days after Election Day.

Federal courts in Oregon
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=35830&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvb3JlZ29uLXZvdGVyLXJvbGwtY2xlYW51cC0yLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=268c968acceaea353e51054ab5449db88b31ac85981eedf6757cf33b6b6d6d45],
California and Illinois
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=35799&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdHMtdG8tY2xlYW4tdXAtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=3ffca2533bc2dd25e07948d2d4a3492d4dca7643bff693d9ece8d8bc8133401b]
recently ruled that our lawsuits against those states may proceed,
forcing them to clean their voter rolls.

We announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=35168&F=T&&hash=54a98dc0af766e1e8cc838fce97a5fdf7566055cdbed1702d666a75e847d5956]
in May that its work led to the removal of more than five million
ineligible names from voter rolls nationwide.

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES TO REVEAL BIDEN COVER-UP ON IRAN NUCLEAR PROGRAM

The events in Iran today underscore the importance of preventing its
mad rulers from acquiring nuclear weapons, about which the Biden
administration seemed lackadaisical.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit against the
Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) for records
regarding the Biden administration’s failure to produce statutorily
required reports on Iran’s uranium enrichment activities (_Judicial
Watch Inc. v. Office of the Director of National Intelligence_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36873&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtb2RuaS1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDAwODMvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=590ee8f9651eb4b5718fa00f74cf1e130c8cfc68dcbcdb5ac6dc083250c94723
_(No.
1:26-cv-00083-APM)).

The lawsuit also seeks all communications between the Office of the
Director of National Intelligence and the 2024 presidential campaigns
of President Biden and his replacement atop the Democrat ticket, Vice
President Harris, regarding the required reports on the Islamic
Republic’s uranium enrichment.

The Iran Nuclear Weapons Capability and Terrorism Act of 2022 requires
the Director of National Intelligence (DNI) to submit semi-annual
reports to Congress on Iran's nuclear activities, including
enrichment.

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the Office of the Director of National Intelligence failed to respond
to a June 30, 2025, FOIA request for:

* Any and all records concerning, regarding, or relating to the delay
of ODNI’s statutorily required reports regarding significant
enrichment activity by the Islamic Republic of Iran.
* Any and all communications between ODNI and the White House
concerning, regarding, or relating to the production and/or delay of
the above referenced reports.
* Any and all communications between ODNI and the Biden presidential
campaign concerning, regarding, or relating to the production and/or
delay of the above referenced reports.
* Any and all communications between ODNI and the Harris presidential
campaign concerning, regarding, or relating to the production and/or
delay of the above referenced reports.

The Biden administration pledged
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36874&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY25iYy5jb20vMjAyMS8wMi8xOS9qY3BvYS1iaWRlbnMtcmV0dXJuLXRvLXRoZS1pcmFuLW51Y2xlYXItZGVhbC1pcy1nZXR0aW5nLWhhcmRlci5odG1sP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=725ee2311c199f054831fbeba4ec4666ca3d8f2c04f0299eb9824294ece17120]
to engage in diplomacy with Iran and revive the 2015 nuclear deal. The
Biden team also pushed back against international attempts to censure
Iran for its nonproliferation violations, reports said. On May 27,
2024, _The Wall Street Journal_ reported that the United States was
blocking
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36875&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud3NqLmNvbS93b3JsZC9taWRkbGUtZWFzdC91LXMtb3Bwb3Nlcy1ldXJvcGVhbi1wbGFuLXRvLWNlbnN1cmUtaXJhbi1vdmVyLW51Y2xlYXItd29yay04NWFkN2ZjNj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=0208e936e359f06924d9d56b23fe2c2b517c8cef3eea4a2682536eca3709e286]
European states from pursuing a formal censure of Iran at the June
board meeting of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA),
despite a push by the United Kingdom, Germany, and France. According
to the Journal, the “U.S. has pressed a number of other countries to
abstain in a censure vote, saying that is what Washington will do.”
The IAEA ultimately passed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36876&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubnl0aW1lcy5jb20vMjAyNC8wNi8wNS93b3JsZC9taWRkbGVlYXN0L2lyYW4tbnVjbGVhci1pYWVhLmh0bWw/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=5c3ce1d149727768d1be63c5f75f3329aa9da01a0644e2ca2468a5205f0dac1d]
the censure resolution on June 5, 2024.

A July 11, 2024, press release
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36877&F=T&&hash=edc7241c6eac7702c46651cf663ae134370d1b8b376175f03b95af372f983738]
from Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) stated that then-Director of National
Intelligence Avril Haines had failed to produce multiple reports on
Iran’s nuclear threat, including uranium enrichment, that are
required by law. Biden dropped out of the presidential race on July
21, 2024, and endorsed Harris, who became the party’s nominee on
August 5, 2024.

In March of last year, the Trump administration’s Office of the
Director of National Intelligence reported in its Annual Threat
Assessment that Iran “possesses the highest uranium enrichment
levels of any non-nuclear state and remains capable of enriching
uranium to weapons-grade levels.”

Weeks after President Trump ordered the "Midnight Hammer" operation on
June 22, 2025, in which the United States Air Force and Navy attacked
three Iranian nuclear facilities, Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan, the
White House assessed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36878&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9hYmNuZXdzLmdvLmNvbS9Qb2xpdGljcy9pcmFuLXByb2R1Y2UtbnVjbGVhci13ZWFwb24tY291cGxlLXdlZWtzLXdoaXRlLWhvdXNlL3N0b3J5P2lkPTEyMzAxNjYzOCZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=d3113fbfb8978b6fc945532c1a3befe78d3e475a6c1a52962f789e828b5da4d4]
that Iran before the attack had the capacity to enrich enough material
for a nuclear weapon within weeks if it chose to do so.

Our lawsuit is aimed at finding out why, as Iran was expanding its
dangerous nuclear weapons program, the Biden-Harris administration
failed to produce these vital reports documenting a clear and present
danger to the United States.

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES OVER FIRING OF ASSOCIATE PRINCIPAL OVER PROTECTED
SPEECH

We filed a federal lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36847&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2JlcmdvbnppLXYtYmFybnN0YWJsZS1wdWJsaWMtc2Nob29scy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMTAwNTkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f42ff3ac9899f891f668f98e29f20e5560f5b915c9e7b9ce88b4bf6d7848b8d7]
against Barnstable Public Schools in Massachusetts for violating the
First Amendment rights of a former associate principal who was fired
over lawful social media posts addressing matters of public concern.

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Massachusetts
on behalf of John Bergonzi, alleging retaliation for protected speech
in violation of 42 U.S.C. § 1983, as well as breach of contract and
promissory estoppel (_Bergonzi v. Barnstable Public Schools_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36847&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2JlcmdvbnppLXYtYmFybnN0YWJsZS1wdWJsaWMtc2Nob29scy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMTAwNTkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f42ff3ac9899f891f668f98e29f20e5560f5b915c9e7b9ce88b4bf6d7848b8d7]
(No. 1:26-cv-10059)).

Bergonzi is a long-time public school educator who left a tenured
teaching position after being hired as an associate principal at
Barnstable High School for the 2024–2025 school year. Prior to
extending an offer of employment, Barnstable Public Schools told
Bergonzi multiple times that no employment offer would be extended
unless and until the school district reviewed his social media
activity, including his Facebook page, for any issues or concerns. It
was explained to him that a social media check was a routine part of
the district’s interview process.

After Bergonzi completed the onboarding process, resigned from his
prior job, and began work, the school district fired him for Facebook
posts he had shared before he was hired. According to the complaint,
the Facebook posts addressed matters of public concern, were made in
Bergonzi’s capacity as a private citizen, and did not identify him
as a Barnstable Public Schools employee.

According to the lawsuit:

> As of the December 10, 2024 termination, Barnstable Public Schools
> had not identified any disturbance caused by [Bergonzi’s] posts.
> Other than the single email from the individual whose name was
> withheld from [Bergonzi], Barnstable Public Schools also had not
> identified any instance in which a parent, student, co-worker, or
> member of the public raised any concern about [Bergonzi’s] posts,
> his social media activity, or his fitness to serve as an associate
> principal. Nor did Barnstable Public Schools identify any concern
> about [Bergonzi’s] work performance or professionalism.

Bergonzi, the complaint alleges, was fired because his protected
speech did not “reflect the values of Barnstable Public Schools. As
a result of his termination, Bergonzi has suffered lost income and
benefits, reputational harm, emotional distress, and diminished
employment prospects.

Public schools do not get to silence employees simply because they
express opinions that administrators dislike. This lawsuit seeks to
hold Barnstable Public Schools accountable for violating the First
Amendment and for reneging on promises that cost our client his
tenured career.

In November 2021, we filed a federal civil rights lawsuit on behalf of
Kari MacRae, a Massachusetts high school teacher who was fired in
retaliation for posts on social media objecting to the inclusion of
critical race theory in schools (_MacRae vs. Matthew Mattos and
Matthew A. Ferron_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=31803&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL21hY3JhZS12LW1hdHRvcy1jb21wbGFpbnQtMTE5MTcvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=92ea8631c1c7d64eb28432acc1493a6bb7b26e5228c6573ff58ed75396c3f9eb

JUDICIAL WATCH TAKES CASE OF TEACHER FIRED FOR SPEECH TO SUPREME COURT

Public employees don’t lose the right of free speech just because
administrators don’t agree with them, and we’re in the U.S.
Supreme Court to make this clear.

We filed a petition for a _writ of certiorari_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36840&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2hlZGdlcGV0aC12LWJyaXR0b24tZXQtYWwtc2NvdHVzLWNlcnQtMjAyNi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ba595d88a23430c284a1b9d75fe613ad7f1bad043fd20bd5470926996589dcae]
asking the Supreme Court of the United States to review a decision of
the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit that permits public
school officials to fire a tenured teacher for political speech made
privately, off duty, and far removed from the classroom.

The case arises from the termination of Jeanne Hedgepeth, a longtime
social studies teacher at Palatine High School in Illinois, who was
fired after posting commentary on her private Facebook page during
summer vacation in 2020, addressing riots and civil unrest following
the death of George Floyd. School administrators cited concerns about
“disruption” after receiving complaints—largely from members
of the public with no direct connection to the school. Hedgepeth
praised Thomas Sowell and other black conservative leaders in one of
the posts that got her fired.

Our 2021 lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=26365&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY2l2aWwtcmlnaHRzLWxhd3N1aXQtdGVhY2hlci8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=6d6e0611cfab12ea203e155a28a6c91416fda52e2381015b3ea94389ac5debac]
for
Hedgepeth asks for damages from the school district, Township High
School District 211, and district board members and officials who
participated directly in the firing of Hedgepeth (_Hedgepeth v.
Britton et al_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=34087&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2hlZGdlcGV0aC12LWJyaXR0b24tY2hpY2Fnby10ZWFjaGVyLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMzc5MC9dXy5fP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=c97dfb5fe8249405e7bc65328b15135cd950819bbabb0a659ce0510a79a1fb1e
(No. 1:21-cv-03890)).

Our _cert_ petition challenges the Seventh Circuit’s decision, which
held that the school district’s interest in “avoiding
disruption” — specifically, emails and phone calls from members of
the public (most of whom had no direct connection to the school)
“expressing concern or outrage” about Hedgepeth’s summer
vacation posts — “outweighs her right to speak.”

Under long-standing Supreme Court precedent, beginning with _Pickering
v. Board of Education_, courts must balance a public employee’s
right to speak on matters of public concern against the government
employer’s interest in maintaining the effective operation of the
workplace. We argue that _Pickering_ does not permit public employers
to punish employees based on disagreement with their political views:

> The viewpoint discrimination in this case is unmistakable. The
> district fired Hedgepeth because members of the community objected
> to the political views she expressed in core First Amendment speech
> on her private Facebook page while on summer vacation. Rather than
> condemn that blatant censorship, the court below sanctioned
> it—holding that vague and unsupported claims of ‘disruption’
> empower public school officials to silence disfavored viewpoints.

The petition further warns that the Seventh Circuit’s decision poses
a serious threat to free speech nationwide:

> If allowed to stand, the decision threatens to chill the political
> speech of millions of public employees nationwide. It teaches that
> lawful, off-duty speech on matters of public concern may be punished
> whenever enough people complain. That rule cannot be squared with
> _Pickering_, with this Court’s precedents, or with the First
> Amendment itself.

We argue that the Seventh Circuit misapplied _Pickering_ by
transforming a narrow balancing test into a broad license for
censorship—allowing government employers to discipline employees not
for workplace misconduct, but for expressing controversial political
opinions that provoke opposition.

This case goes to the heart of whether the First Amendment still
protects 22 million public employees from being fired for daring to
exercise their God-given First Amendment right to express views
outside the workplace. No teacher should lose her career because
outsiders object to political views she expressed privately and
lawfully.

We are being assisted in the case by former U.S. Solicitor General
Paul Clement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78991&L=36841&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY2xlbWVudG11cnBoeS5jb20vd2hvLXdlLWFyZS9wYXVsLWNsZW1lbnQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=03907d859e27950d65336b8ba5888d34fc5f7d719aa03a2759ed7c01ef9946f3],
who has argued over 100 cases before the U.S. Supreme Court.
