name: Talarico for Texas
subject: Budget decisions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
**Budget decisions.**
We are just weeks away from the Democratic primary for United States Senate in Texas.
From the outside, you see our rallies, our block walks and phone banks, and the pundits on television talking about our race.
But inside HQ, we’re looking at maps, spreadsheets, and difficult budget decisions.
The truth is, if we don’t meet our budget goals each week, it means something has to get cut — whether it’s a town hall where James gets to meet with voters face to face, or one less week on TV sharing James’ message with voters on the airwaves.
The result of this primary campaign will come down to how far we can get the word out about James. It's already a close race, and every single dollar is going to count.
But what you may not know is that emails like this one are one of the most important ways we raise money. That means we need people who care about flipping Texas' Senate seat to step up now — before it's too late.
**[Please make today the day you make your first contribution to James Talarico's campaign for United States Senate in Texas. We will win this race if people like you care enough to make it happen.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q1.AKJ3HX&refcode=em260117-1-ak&t=1&akid=393%2E1347274%2EtOxUhs)**
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._

James Talarico for Senate

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: 153 Democrats voted with Trump. Help us unseat 4 of them →
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

This week, 153 House Democrats joined Republicans in voting to
rubber-stamp Donald Trump’s far-right agenda, including:

* Another $3.3 billion for Israel’s military as it continues to violate
the ceasefire with its genocide in Gaza.
* A complete ban on U.S. funding for UNRWA, the largest source of
humanitarian assistance for Palestinians, and any international bodies
investigating Israel’s actions, like the ICC.
* Funding for regime change in Venezuela.
* Reforms to help billionaires evade tax enforcement from the IRS.
* Codifying anti-abortion policies.

Do-Nothing Democratic incumbents Shri Thanedar, Jimmy Gomez, Diana
DeGette, and Wesley Bell sold out their communities to the Trump
administration and voted for this.

Justice Democrats Donavan McKinney, Angela Gonzalez-Torres, Melat Kiros,
and Cori Bush are running to unseat them in this year’s primaries.

As Melat said, “Corporate Democrats will always put their donors above
their voters. It’s time to clean house and vote every single one of them
out.”

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260117.L4l8F4&refcode=em20260117-37377&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37377%2E1203867%2EpqiJJn ]Rush $5 or more to back our progressive, working-class
challengers and replace spineless Democrats who sell out their communities
→

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260117.L4l8F4&refcode=em20260117-37377&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1021&akid=37377%2E1203867%2EpqiJJn ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

body:
You can’t make this stuff up.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123506112/595674568/-153530995?refcode=EM_F_260114fake_N_SB_90DO_6_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZDhkZWYxNC1iM2YzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eT2a85bqrOzVpr9jogcj_nuoYdPdBEU5_Z4IsYta7Tc=) Peter,

Despite some Republicans reaching across the aisle to vote with Democrats on addressing the GOP-created healthcare crisis, **Trump said he would veto the bill.**

This comes as, during a stump speech in Michigan, Trump stated that affordability “is a fake word by Democrats.”

_You can’t make this stuff up._

You know what’s not fake? Due to Republican inaction, millions of Americans across the nation have seen their healthcare premiums skyrocket.

While extremist Republicans want you to think they have done everything they can to address rising costs, they haven’t done a single thing. And are instead standing by as Trump threatens to kill a bill that would _finally_ address healthcare costs becoming inaccessible to millions.

[**We can’t stand idly by as Republicans continue to sow chaos and division while people are forced to decide between paying their mortgage or paying for their medicine. **Donald Trump just accepted a $2.5 million bribe to overturn my work as a federal prosecutor.** As a federal prosecutor, I led the investigation into Julio Herrera Velutini’s illegal bribery of the former Governor of Puerto Rico. Then, just as Herrera Velutini was at his criminal trial, his daughter made a $2.5 million contribution to MAGA Inc., Donald Trump’s super PAC. Yesterday, Trump pardoned both the former governor and Herrera Velutini. This is the cycle of the Trump administration’s corruption: criminal cronyism, a donation, a pardon, and all of it completely out in the open. **This open sale of justice occurred SEPARATELY from the Eric Adams case, where the Trump administration asked me to drop a case for political reasons. I refused, because my oath was to the Constitution and the rule of law, not the President.** **I have trained prosecutors worldwide on prosecuting corrupt public officials. And I can tell you that it doesn’t take long before corruption becomes a part of a nation’s culture. We are running out of time to stop the damage being done.** That’s why I’m running for Congress — to fight for a justice system that serves the public, not the powerful. [To end this corruption, we must flip PA-07. Will you chip in $15 right now to help fuel our fight?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsv9ThOZuJ_kb7XILY6U0n15h3qseHtL_y2nItmhz2Xj9biD-6iBwVCTHc32wvioKuWK5Z5Viqy_0miE-DFif-IESY5ndBOZzAIpRw3M9KmJPHCisjgjCdWB8M8bOeiBXf2U1rw05DkUnSXmgbirBc5A60dGIVSqW3U9FL0hj9txU6ChcpHGoN8PJ4u33_kHvPNdqwxB4vOhPKDjJvsssStAS6W109EjTh1Z0OdXho_ld9Q1jLXKYcencxSe_4XWziNceG1eA9Ae1bx_IlRJS15JQ2g2L1CAqyrHCmmPv3mqd1RmKd_Msbdqx3rpbAPojuWYuciPjfF2CHFbv5l3VQHSK6uJexLyCzE_DEBNbiW2cNcyvW_p2SNSt3CqaAn927BT5YcceGlPYT-wzHC-_O8/4nd/M7YJqwsBRh6D-Opo8ibVgg/h1/h001.aaxRxZ2jqUN2KlP-j48M8QGoSHAe3mgfXB_SQc0zBCY) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

As we hit the ground running in 2026, Janelle wants to hear from you and ensure she’s working to address the issues that matter most to you and your family in Congress. subject: An oil billionaire from Texas is trying to buy our Senate seat.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
Here’s what’s happening:

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1924%2E49239%2EGlT7ip)

**null,** **we have some news out of Michigan:** An oil billionaire from Texas is trying to buy our Senate seat. He poured $5 million into a super PAC backing MAGA Republican Mike Rogers.
It’s Team Haley, and here’s what’s happening:
💸 **In the first half of 2025, more than 98% of the money** behind this pro-Rogers super PAC came from a Texan named Tim Dunn – an ultra-wealthy oil guy and outspoken Christian nationalist who believes that only Christians should hold leadership roles.
🚨 Dunn doesn’t live in Michigan. He’s not trying to help Michigan working families. He’s trying to buy power across the country and push his far-right agenda in the Senate.
📊 Polls show **Haley Stevens is the only Democrat** consistently beating Mike Rogers – and this race will likely decide control of the U.S. Senate.
**Here's the takeaway:** A Big Oil, MAGA megadonor is bankrolling a rightwing takeover of Michigan. But our grassroots campaign is fighting back – and we need your help.
[**That’s why we set a critical $15,000 daily goal we need to hit to prove this seat is not for sale. Will you rush $5 right now to help Haley keep Michigan blue?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11711?t=3&akid=1924%2E49239%2EGlT7ip)
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11711?t=4&akid=1924%2E49239%2EGlT7ip)

Thank you for standing with us, null.

Team Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11715?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=8&akid=1924%2E49239%2EGlT7ip) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11710?t=9&akid=1924%2E49239%2EGlT7ip)

Thank you,
Haley

name: Mike Johnson, 56th Speaker of the House
subject: For Peter only
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement.

Hello – it’s Eileen Laubacher, the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Admiral running against Lauren Boebert – [**and in a moment, I am going to ask you to make a monthly donation to my campaign to help me defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat blue. But before I do, please let me explain why your donation today is so important.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123507792/595680466/156431970?refcode=20260117am-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGQwYmU4MmYtYjVmMy1mMDExLThkNGMtMDAwZDNhNTRiNTYwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dyEuBgyKio1-vqAr-wf_bKEk0CStto2vD-hAOJNEW_c=)

But when I see our constitution and our democracy under attack the way they are right now by people like Lauren Boebert, there was no answer other than to try to serve once again.
We can defeat Lauren Boebert and finally end her embarrassing political career. Adam Frisch only lost to Boebert by 546 votes in what was the closest race in the entire country. She is not invincible.
And if we do defeat Boebert and flip this seat blue, Democrats are basically guaranteed to take back the House this November!
But Lauren Boebert has taken millions of dollars from corporate PACs and the likes of Elon Musk. **If we are going to be able to fight back against the Super PAC lies and win, I am counting on grassroots donors like you to chip in to my campaign now.**

clean_body: Hello – it’s Eileen Laubacher, the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Admiral running against Lauren Boebert. Hello – it’s Eileen Laubacher, the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Admiral running against Lauren Boebert – = I never thought I’d run for Congress. After 34 years in the Navy, I retired planning to spend a lot more time with my husband and our kids. But when I see our constitution and our democracy under attack the way they are right now by people like Lauren Boebert, there was no answer other than to try to serve once again. We can defeat Lauren Boebert and finally end her embarrassing political career. Adam Frisch only lost to Boebert by 546 votes in what was the closest race in the entire country. She is not invincible. And if we do defeat Boebert and flip this seat blue, Democrats are basically guaranteed to take back the House this November! But Lauren Boebert has taken millions of dollars from corporate PACs and the likes of Elon Musk. If we are going to be able to fight back against the Super PAC lies and win, I am counting on grassroots donors like you to chip in to my campaign now. = Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = We can finally give Lauren Boebert the boot – but we can only do it together. * * *
Friend, Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem just went on national television and knowingly lied about an ICE agent killing Renee Nicole Good — again. The video evidence is clear. Renee was calm. She smiled. She deescalated. And then she was shot in the face by an ICE officer. But that hasn’t stopped Noem from labeling her a “domestic terrorist,” blaming Renee for her own death, and spreading provably false narratives to justify a brutal killing. This is not just dishonest to the American people. It’s gaslighting at the highest level of government.**And it cannot stand.** [**Democrats are moving to impeach and remove Noem from office at this very moment. name: ICE Must Face Justice via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Demand accountability for Renee Good’s murder
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Right now, Trump and his cronies are spreading lies and disinformation to
help ensure the ICE agent who murdered Renee Good will walk free. But
progressive champions like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Ro Khanna are
demanding justice – and they need ALL of us to stand with them.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34941?source=email-20260117&t=1011&akid=10090%2E5885482%2E9Ifv7N ]Headline: Democrats call for ICE officer in Minneapolis shooting to be
prosecuted

AOC said it best: "What we saw today is a murder, and murders in cold
blood need to be prosecuted." Without accountability, ICE agents will keep
acting with impunity, terrorizing families, neighbors, and every single
one of our communities.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34941?source=email-20260117&t=1012&akid=10090%2E5885482%2E9Ifv7N ]We must stand behind these calls for justice with massive public
pressure. Add your name now – demand the immediate arrest and prosecution
of the ICE officer who killed Renee Good.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34941?source=email-20260117&t=1013&akid=10090%2E5885482%2E9Ifv7N ]ADD YOUR NAME

After Renee Good’s murder, federal agencies pushed Minnesota authorities
out of the investigation, blocking state investigators from access to
evidence.

But state officials aren’t letting this case go. They’re launching their
own review to ensure every shred of truth comes to light – video footage,
witness statements, and all the facts federal agents wanted to bury.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34941?source=email-20260117&t=1014&akid=10090%2E5885482%2E9Ifv7N ]Headline: Minnesota to review ICE shooting after FBI blocks state
agents from case

This isn’t just about one officer or one department – this is about
whether people in power get to crush our rights, murder our loved ones,
and walk away with a handshake and a pension. Because without
accountability today, ICE will continue acting with impunity. And
tomorrow’s tragedy will be another name, another heartbreak, another
family destroyed.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34941?source=email-20260117&t=1015&akid=10090%2E5885482%2E9Ifv7N ]Together let’s drown out Trump’s lies with our call for justice. Sign

subject: The Voting Rights Act is in danger
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
We must be ready to defend fair elections after the decision on Louisiana v. Callais.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123508234/595681891/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImUxMGJlODJmLWI1ZjMtZjAxMS04ZDRjLTAwMGQzYTU0YjU2MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CyCoGDSRdYVUInDpTtQP4KVCh3Sq53Qnf_TOIgoW72o=&emci=a3b61860-08f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=e10be82f-b5f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=8431045)

Peter, **The Voting Rights Act is in danger of being gutted ahead of this year’s midterms. ** The Supreme Court’s pending decision on _Louisiana v. Callais_ has the potential to open the floodgates on President Trump’s redistricting crisis by completely disenfranchising black and brown voters — and upending so much progress for fair representation. _Louisiana v. Callais_ focuses on Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, which is “the central safeguard against racially discriminatory redistricting.” [**It’s up to us to be ready when the Supreme Court makes its decision, which could happen any day now. We need your support to scale our resources to mobilize, no matter how the Court rules.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123508237/595681894/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AbramsQuote_email_20260117-NDRC-Tier1-AbramsQuote_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260117-NDRC-Tier1-AbramsQuote_&utm_campaign=AbramsQuote&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImUxMGJlODJmLWI1ZjMtZjAxMS04ZDRjLTAwMGQzYTU0YjU2MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CyCoGDSRdYVUInDpTtQP4KVCh3Sq53Qnf_TOIgoW72o=) Here’s how Stacey Abrams framed what’s at stake: | _**“This decision could undo decades of hard-won progress on civil rights for our communities and the consequences are clear.” **_

_\- Stacey Abrams_
---
We know there are Republican lawmakers banking on this decision coming down with enough time to pass new, unfair maps ahead of the midterms. Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis has openly talked about preparing for that outcome — the NDRC is the organization created to fight back and defend fair representation for everyone.
[**Make a donation today to ensure that our fair maps movement has all the support we can get to defeat extreme anti-democracy attacks on voting rights.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123508240/595681897/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AbramsQuote_email_20260117-NDRC-Tier1-AbramsQuote_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260117-NDRC-Tier1-AbramsQuote_&utm_campaign=AbramsQuote&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImUxMGJlODJmLWI1ZjMtZjAxMS04ZDRjLTAwMGQzYTU0YjU2MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=CyCoGDSRdYVUInDpTtQP4KVCh3Sq53Qnf_TOIgoW72o=)
Thank you for showing up for fair elections across the country,
Team NDRC

body:
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001XvBc3bsHgRWXLyJfHoB0-w%3D&ch=005337f2-e4bf-11f0-bd14-0242b661c010&ca=8fe6dff0-319b-4b8c-9309-7b39f7522a4b) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.

Peter, I recently visited Sedgwick Health center in Julesburg, Colorado. They serve seniors, run the local nursing home and keep their ER open around the clock. According to the hospital, 80% of their patients rely on Medicaid and Medicare. But now that the GOP's "Big, Beautiful Bill" has become law, Sedgwick will lose 25% of its Medicaid reimbursement - and all of their services might not survive it. Lauren Boebert voted for this. She voted to gut the care her constituents depend on. I know firsthand how critical accessible health care is for Coloradan families. I fought tooth and nail to get my parents the care they needed when they were both diagnosed with cancer. No one should have to beg for help when they're sick or struggling - but that's exactly the reality Boebert just voted for.
As one rural hospital CEO put it: "Where you live shouldn't determine if you live." He's right. And I'll never stop working to ensure our neighbors get the care they need - no matter their ZIP code. That's why I'm on the road - meeting with hospital leaders, families and local officials who know what's really at stake. But this kind of campaign takes resources, Seth. Every trip we take, every ad we place, every door we knock on - it's all powered by grassroots folks like you. [So I have to ask: Will you pitch in $25 or more before midnight to help me beat Boebert, flip this seat and stand up for Coloradans?](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ewpQ_8vTtay5rCrQMKkwQbuDsg0NuOojWwJ8p6ExPQYXv7GVqKwVj5JSV9tdfShQhpuHY070Ef5bl7L-Ryh4iAF5e2w70MCRcrQzUFwBy4HoPEAkm_KmAHh-gyCuq6VOHKlLZATvkLrAolULsEAK5Ael3CQB_osQ1rElGkkBd7Y-IFHAUMc3uEn_WhBVYB4EyuTlZV2uf4Kag246H2HfV_tMhnycP4W-kw8nazhCsvk=&c=d8qhmdkuyJwAFSUoTBWTLqssWpPsuGtP_KgZMMVRM_e7wyYb67rQUA==&ch=sSCHZ9iPig_dDitnIUws3z1xxg3ejVMJw3yI0-wPwoQdb0hwzkJYqA==)
---

clean_body: | | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | | == | | == Peter, I recently visited Sedgwick Health center in Julesburg, Colorado. They serve seniors, run the local nursing home and keep their ER open around the clock. According to the hospital, 80% of their patients rely on Medicaid and Medicare. But now that the GOP's "Big, Beautiful Bill" has become law, Sedgwick will lose 25% of its Medicaid reimbursement - and all of their services might not survive it. Lauren Boebert voted for this. She voted to gut the care her constituents depend on. I know firsthand how critical accessible health care is for Coloradan families. I fought tooth and nail to get my parents the care they needed when they were both diagnosed with cancer. No one should have to beg for help when they're sick or struggling - but that's exactly the reality Boebert just voted for. As one rural hospital CEO put it: "Where you live shouldn't determine if you live." He's right. And I'll never stop working to ensure our neighbors get the care they need - no matter their ZIP code. That's why I'm on the road - meeting with hospital leaders, families and local officials who know what's really at stake. But this kind of campaign takes resources, Seth. Every trip we take, every ad we place, every door we knock on - it's all powered by grassroots folks like you. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Thanks for believing in this campaign. # [The Verizon Outage](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=184870626&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0ODcwNjI2LCJpYXQiOjE3Njg2NjI2NjksImV4cCI6MTc3MTI1NDY2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.T4AU9SHInqOLJbC2YMh2613e_E9ImCuoWAxUk6GggTg)

I hope that your week has been great. I had a wonderful week celebrating my birthday with family and friends.
Millions of people lost their Verizon service this week on Wednesday. Why did this happen? "A software issue." Verizon issued an apology but specifics have yet to emerge. They did offer a $20 rebate to those who requested it, which hasn't gotten great feedback.
I received dozens of messages Wednesday from friends on Verizon:
"Infuriating."
"I can't do anything."
"Nothing works."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2422a43b-d814-4705-86f9-9d6b7cb2059a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I was a Verizon customer for 25 years before switching to Noble Mobile a few months ago. I will say, all of my stuff worked perfectly fine this week as was the case for everyone on Noble, which sits on the T-Mobile network.
Not coincidentally, Noble saw a surge in signups on Thursday and Friday from now-former Verizon subscribers who said they saw the outage as a sign to switch.
They were motivated by a few things.
First, Verizon is expensive. The average American is paying $83/month for their wireless connection; I was paying Verizon $150 a month for just my one line. Why did I pay so much? I convinced myself that Verizon was 'the best network.' But can it be the best if it goes down without warning? Why pay a premium if it's not going to be reliable?
Second, the natural fear is that if there's an outage, there could be another one, particularly as there hasn't been a detailed explanation for this one.
These fears are well-founded. Lee McKnight, an Information Studies professor at Syracuse said that outages are "a fact of life these days for major telecommunications firms. Modern telecom networks are cloud networks. 5G networks are mainly, like, hundreds of different cloud services." Hence the software issue. Sanjoy Paul, a wireless network expert at Rice University echoed this, saying "With cloud and software-based networks, there are more opportunities for glitches and attacks . . . small issues with computer code can have big consequences."
Should one be concerned about Verizon in particular? Well, the company is going through a lot of change. They got a new CEO in October, who said that "cost reductions will be a way of life." They then immediately announced layoffs of 13,000 employees, which is about 13% of their workforce. That's not great. The new guy immediately said, though, that he wouldn't touch Verizon's 6.6% cash dividend, which pays out about $11 billion a year to shareholders. That dividend is a nice reward for holding the stock, but it doesn't do a lot for network reliability.
One thing I noticed too: Verizon recently had a marketing campaign that said, "2-year price guarantee" as in "we won't raise your price for 2 years if you sign up now." Which kind of suggests that . . . they plan on raising prices.
Let's say you're someone who thinks you're paying for quality and reliability. My argument to Verizon customers is that, at this point, you're probably paying more to enrich Verizon shareholders than you are anything else. Is that worth paying more every month?
Also, a lot of the people I spoke to this week really value being connected. Did you know you can add a second network on your phone via an eSim for $30 a month? Any iPhone from 13 on has this capacity, as do the newer Samsung and Google devices. That way, if your network goes down, you could just switch networks. This week, when you noticed your phone wasn't working, you could have switched to the Noble eSim and been perfectly fine. You could even use this as a way to get comfortable with a new network.
Evelyn said to me, "It might be a good idea for different family members to be on different networks in case of an emergency." Two might be better than one.
In this day and age, that might be the lesson from this week's outage. Things will go down. Redundancy might be a very good thing. And if you're paying for something, you should do your best to see that you're getting what you paid for.
To switch to Noble to cut your wireless bill and be rewarded for using less data, email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/0495eda5-e459-4cf1-a89e-9a16bdfcbddc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for our best offer, which is [3 free months](https://substack.com/redirect/c609726b-629d-42d6-9294-43852bcca351?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). You can also email us to add a second esim to your phone. This won't be the last outage. Why not be prepared? You'll feel very smart that day.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a3703a79-63ad-4577-a4d5-691cdfff1164?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

And if you're paying for something, you should do your best to see that you're getting what you paid for. To switch to Noble to cut your wireless bill and be rewarded for using less data, email for our best offer, which is . You can also email us to add a second esim to your phone. This won't be the last outage. Why not be prepared? You'll feel very smart that day. | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Andrew Yang 111 Sutter Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

Last Wednesday, 37-year old Renee Good was shot in the face by Jonathan Ross, a federal ICE agent who--like any number of his colleagues--has been effectively empowered to stand above the law and disregard basic human and civil rights.
Renee was a mother, a poet, and a U.S. citizen. She committed no crime, posed no threat at all to law enforcement personnel, and died less than a mile from where George Floyd was murdered by a police officer in 2020.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/454a55e3-abd3-45b7-8716-2adf5067f48e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The more things change, the more they stay the same.
Beyond being a common turn of phrase, that saying carries more truth & poignance in this moment than casual observation might suggest.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! Help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=184841412&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0ODQxNDEyLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg2NjMzNTksImV4cCI6MTc3MTI1NTM1OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.LGMTDGI_ooIUgeTCEh2F0PiQWuQvDCi3CpsvwJTein8)
### **Murder in broad daylight**
Various officials lied in the wake of Good's death about the circumstances of her murder by ICE agent Jonathan Ross, including the president, Attorney General Pam Bondi, and Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem. Contrary to their false claims, Renee was turning away from Ross when he shot her, and was polite up until the moment of her death.
Good's last words were, "I'm not mad at you, dude." Her words were so poignant that artist Maria De Victoria has taken to [reciting](https://substack.com/redirect/fa1a969c-7ec5-49fc-9b21-e6383f4fcc2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) them outside the ICE field office in downtown Manhattan.
Ross, in sharp contrast, revealed his state of mind when walking away from the scene of his crime. His words were revealing, "Fucking bitch."
Setting aside the obvious misogyny reflected by his words, his actions were plainly criminal. No statute authorizes lethal violence by ICE agents without any reasonable basis, while any number of legal authorities--from the constitutional requirement for due process to Minnesota's criminal laws--clearly prohibit it.
Ultimately, there is no legal basis for ICE agents to be granted any degree of immunity for the wanton violence they continue to spread across the United States.
### **Gaslighting the public**
In the wake of her murder, Good was smeared relentlessly by each of the unrepentant liars in office who defended Ross, the ICE officer who shot Good.
Trump, Bondi, and Noem each claimed that Ross was injured, and suffered internal bleeding, even though video from the event--seen by billions of people--makes clear that Ross was unharmed, and that Good was attempting to comply with Ross' directives when he shot her for no reason.
Officials are gaslighting the public in multiple dimensions.
The president deployed ICE to Minneapolis to respond to a supposed crime wave, which was essentially an ironic fabrication: not only was Minneapolis relatively peaceful before the ICE deployment, but the public safety crisis that has erupted since has been a direct consequence of violence and provocation by ICE agents.
In that context, misrepresenting the circumstances of Good's death adds layers of dystopia upon the initial deployment of ICE and Good's murder. Official efforts to distort reality have been so pervasive that White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt launched into what foreign journalists described as a "[tirade](https://substack.com/redirect/f6e16978-dd1b-4c70-ab0e-4d61b482ff18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" when asked about it by Niall Stanage, a White House columnist for the Hill.
Leavitt's response to Stanage's question is revealing:
> OK, so you're a biased reporter with a leftwing opinion ... Because you're a leftwing hack. You're not a reporter, posing in this room as a journalist. And it's so clear by the premise of your question. And you and the people of the media who have such biases but fake like you're a journalist, you shouldn't even be sitting in that seat."
The sheer belligerence of Leavitt's response matches the tone set by, and seems to reflect the same [fragility](https://substack.com/redirect/b7ca7bfa-6c06-4b37-b5a0-efa32e3dbba1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) exhibited by, her boss. Outrageously, multiple news outlets have supported her characterization--including not only FOX News (which should surprise no one), but also CBS, which separately ran a [fabricated story](https://substack.com/redirect/18092392-a143-4218-a89d-52bd1edffb08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about ICE agent Jonathan Ross' implausible internal injuries that prompted [outrage](https://substack.com/redirect/9304b417-303f-4f51-8153-be246abdce39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) within the network's news department.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication made possible by readers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work and an independent news ecosystem, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..IKjRTAHPbSiQjpDGxUkAfahdpf_EY4E8NIXL7G_c1nM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=184841412)
### **What it means**
This is what martial law looks like.
The admin's defense of a murder in broad daylight reflects an attempt from the highest levels of government to normalize impunity.
I've long had a bug in my bonnet about executive impunity.
Before I ran for Congress, my most visible public moment came when I was [arrested](https://substack.com/redirect/cf77e405-ca38-4c38-aea1-fa884a4e961a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S. Senate in 2015 for asking a question of an Obama official who lied to Congress under oath about the scope & scale of mass surveillance.
There's a reason that accountability matters for executive officials who violate the law. Without accountability, restraints on executive power are effectively meaningless.
Those restraints, meanwhile, perform a crucial public function: namely, defending individual rights from government overreach.
In order for our rights to mean anything, officials need to face justice when they violate them. Lying to the Senate under oath about a matter of grave constitutional importance should be much more than enough to justify a criminal charge. Even though committed by people wearing suits, lies to legislative bodies are among the greatest conceivable crimes against the public.
But rarely, if ever, are those crimes prosecuted.
With no reason beyond convenience and opportunism, our "justice" system tends to focus on the least powerful individuals, effectively inviting more serious crimes at scale to continue indefinitely.
Will Noem or Trump ever face justice for attempting to justify the murder of a U.S. citizen in broad daylight?
Don't hold your breath. Bondi is the nation's chief law enforcement officer, and she's part of the gang.
Worse yet, whatever pretense of impartiality Americans imagine they might receive from the courts is equally chimerical. Neither the intra-executive nor the inter-branch checks on executive power are operating under this administration, which is why the president is effectively operating without any meaningful restraint.
### **Things stay the same**
None of this is new.
If anything, the era of racial terror across the South was far worse. Lynchings left hundreds of Black men dead, and most of them endured merciless torture before losing their lives.
At least Renee Good died quickly, and presumably in less pain than Emmitt Till and less anguish than Fred Hampton.
She presumably died in less torment than the tens of thousands of [indigenous](https://substack.com/redirect/db75ab89-3fdc-41d6-8140-620364955da0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Americans murdered for bounties and land.
Does that somehow indicate progress?
Today, even white women are being murdered by the state.
Long among the most reliable instigators of racial terror, white women became demographically synonymous during the COVID pandemic with fragility and racial weaponization. The way that the name Karen became reduced to an epithet reflects this understanding.
Yet white women have now fallen overtly victim to the "security" apparatus built in response to public "security" concerns. Does it matter to anyone that ICE was built to supposedly counter immigration, and is now murdering U.S. citizens--even white women--with impunity?
The co-optation of legitimate concerns to enable illegitimate official responses is even older than the Republic itself. The emergence of ICE as a domestic terror organization [continues](https://open.substack.com/pub/wkamaubell/p/can-america-please-learn-the-lesson?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios) and expands on a pattern that took root decades ago in the South.
Had America bothered to take action when the killing spree was confined to people of color, we might have all enjoyed nice things, like basic human rights. Instead, we tumble into dystopia, with every day offering new horrors as [the mask](https://substack.com/redirect/f5506e75-42e9-4702-a0f3-f32142d5845e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) continues to drop.

### **Martin Luther King 's lament (1/8/26)**
It starts
with the wailing
of dark-skinned
women mourning loss
It leads
to a hunger
for long
denied justice
It ends
with comfortable
allies turning
their heads
until even
they find
themselves
shedding blood
They might find
it difficult
at that point
to put
the blood
back in

subject: One year in, we need your thoughts

body:
You don’t support Trump, do you?!
| [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/grade-trump/?refcode=VPP_20260117_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC&t=6&akid=22617%2E878179%2Egxw-6G) Friend, January 20th marks ONE YEAR since Trump was sworn into his second term as president.

_Here’s a brief reminder of some of the chaos he’s unleashed in just a year:_

❌ He GUTTED Medicaid and sent healthcare costs SKYROCKETING.
❌ He’s MILITARIZING our cities.
❌ He said he “DOESN’T KNOW” if he’ll uphold the Constitution.

**So we want to know what YOU think, Friend — we need to make sure his first major job rating reflects the American people!** | **DONALD TRUMP REPORT CARD** [How would you grade Trump’s first year back in office?](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/grade-trump/?refcode=VPP_20260117_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC&t=7&akid=22617%2E878179%2Egxw-6G) | [F - HORRIBLE »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/grade-trump/?refcode=VPP_20260117_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC&t=8&akid=22617%2E878179%2Egxw-6G)

body:
Can you take my campaign’s 2026 Priorities Survey?

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123510886/595690747/-1720966344?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZThmZDk1ZC1iOWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHEJ4KmTos7FAJVHr8G2PAaeZOm-QubtOwb1zrN1xz0=) Derek,

As we kick off the new year – the year of the midterm elections, I should add – I am asking for something deeply important. And no, it’s not money.

Today, I am asking for your direct input.

**Will you spare a couple minutes to fill out my campaign’s 2026 Priorities Survey, Derek?** **[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123510887/595690750/-963781499?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZThmZDk1ZC1iOWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHEJ4KmTos7FAJVHr8G2PAaeZOm-QubtOwb1zrN1xz0=&emci=e4f0a885-fff2-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=8e8fd95d-b9f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=4568237)** So much has happened in the first couple weeks of the year, I know it feels overwhelming to keep track of the chaos.

Senate Republicans blocked bipartisan, House-passed legislation to extend ACA tax credits and lower costs for millions of Americans... Trump continues to threaten a half-dozen countries across the globe... Nothing is being done by Republicans to address the sky-high cost of living.

But I want to tell you that I’m keeping track of it all, Derek – and I need to know that we’re on the same page as we gear up to fight back in this critical election year.

[_**That’s why I’m asking you to spare two minutes to fill out my campaign’s 2026 Priorities Survey >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123510889/595690751/-963781499?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZThmZDk1ZC1iOWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHEJ4KmTos7FAJVHr8G2PAaeZOm-QubtOwb1zrN1xz0=&emci=e4f0a885-fff2-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=8e8fd95d-b9f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=4568237)

Thank you for taking the time,

Mark Warner **Before you go:**

Republicans will spend whatever it takes to hold onto power, no matter how unpopular their agenda is with voters.

**And they know that there’s no Senate majority for Democrats without Virginia.**

[_**Will you consider a contribution today as an early investment in the campaign infrastructure we're building to reelect Mark Warner and flip control of Congress? Your donation today will have a much bigger impact than a donation of the same amount made right before the midterm elections.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123510891/595690756/-1720966344?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZThmZDk1ZC1iOWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHEJ4KmTos7FAJVHr8G2PAaeZOm-QubtOwb1zrN1xz0=)**__********[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123510892/595690757/-1720966344?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_Priorities_260117_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZThmZDk1ZC1iOWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHEJ4KmTos7FAJVHr8G2PAaeZOm-QubtOwb1zrN1xz0=)**********

subject: Did you forget about this?
body:
You haven’t responded

| | | | Peter,
We’re following up with you. A couple of days ago, we sent you an email offering you our exclusive [GOP Platinum Membership](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-p_r6gYZHbLGA).
We only invite our top supporters to join this exclusive club, and you made the cut! So, imagine our reaction when we found out you still HAVEN’T accepted your offer.

We’re sure it was just a mistake. Maybe the email got lost in your inbox. Whatever the reason, we’re following up with you here. body:
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2031%2E791427%2EMEYFBR)
Friend, we’re reaching out to humbly ask you to chip in $10 to Ilhan Omar’s campaign for reelection.
**Yes, just $10.**
$10 because Minneapolis and all of Minnesota is today engaged in a historic struggle for civil rights and basic human decency as ICE occupies our state.
$10 because Ilhan has been a consistent leader on opposing ICE’s brutality, defunding this corrupt agency, holding ICE agents criminally accountable for wrongdoing, and impeaching DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.
$10 because despite everything, Ilhan still has a campaign to run and a race to win, and we can’t afford to let our fundraising slip and send a signal to AIPAC that they can swoop in and buy this race.
Ilhan relies on grassroots donations from folks like you. Not billionaires, Super PACs, or corporate lobbyists.
So please: [**Make a contribution of $10 or any other amount today and stand with Ilhan Omar in this important moment for Minnesota. We’ve always been able to count on this grassroots team to show up when we need it most. Our team just set an ambitious fundraising goal, so we’re going to need a lot of help to reach that milestone and build all the momentum we’ll need to win this race and keep Pennsylvania moving forward.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123511611/595693577/-1801658537?amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_011726_B1_SHAPIROSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MjkxMDA3Ni1iYmYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x5UetzWDwUA9X_rfZIppWuD3LQAj8w2FZmeVGivfBM8=) | | **[DONATE →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123511620/595693586/-1801658537?amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_011726_B1_SHAPIROSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MjkxMDA3Ni1iYmYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x5UetzWDwUA9X_rfZIppWuD3LQAj8w2FZmeVGivfBM8=)**

Growing up in Montgomery County, my parents raised me with a core set of values: Work hard. Give back. Stand up for others. And Peter, they led by example. As a pediatrician, my dad helped lead kids and families through the stress of challenging, uncertain moments. And as an educator, my mom taught me and so many other kids to care about the world around us.
Watching their work up close inspired me to follow in their footsteps — but in a different way. Instead of medicine or education, I wanted to help Pennsylvanians through public service. By cleaning up our government, solving problems, and making life better for the families I served.
That’s what I did as a State Representative, when I helped pass some of the toughest ethics laws in Pennsylvania history. It’s what I did as Attorney General, when I got justice for survivors of sexual abuse, protected health care access, and won in court dozens of times to defend our democracy. **And Peter, it’s what I’m doing right now as Pennsylvania’s 48th Governor.**
Together, we’ve cut taxes seven times, putting millions back into the pockets of parents, seniors, and small businesses. We’ve invested a record amount of money in public education and served over 90 million free breakfasts to Pennsylvania students. We’ve created over 18,000 jobs, grown our economy, and expanded opportunity across our Commonwealth. And yes, we’ve protected abortion rights, ensuring every Pennsylvanian has the freedom to access the health care they need.
**But my work isn’t finished.** Today, Pennsylvanians are facing unprecedented challenges with a White House hell bent on raising costs, attacking our freedoms, and undermining our democracy.
And this year, we have the chance to defeat the politics of chaos, division, and extremism that are making it harder across our Commonwealth. We have the opportunity to show the nation that hatred and division are not the way forward. That means standing up to the politicians who enable Donald Trump and JD Vance — even when they know their policies hurt Pennsylvanians.
[Peter, let’s keep moving our Commonwealth forward together. It’s going to take a full team effort here, and the success of our campaign will come down to grassroots supporters like you. So I’m humbly asking: Can you join our team by rushing a split donation between the Democratic Party of Georgia and my campaign and become a Founding Donor?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123511630/595693597/-1801658537?amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_011726_B1_SHAPIROSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MjkxMDA3Ni1iYmYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x5UetzWDwUA9X_rfZIppWuD3LQAj8w2FZmeVGivfBM8=)

clean_body: Today, I’m launching my reelection campaign to keep moving Pennsylvania forward – and keep getting stuff done. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = = Peter, I wanted you to hear it directly from me. I’m officially launching my reelection campaign for Governor of Pennsylvania. Hear me on this: we've gotten a whole lot done, on issues that have been stuck for decades. But there's always more to do. More people to help. More Pennsylvanians to protect. More bridges to build. That’s why I’m running for reelection — to build upon the foundation we’ve laid, put more points on the board for Pennsylvania families, and keep getting sht done. = | | = Growing up in Montgomery County, my parents raised me with a core set of values: Work hard. Give back. Stand up for others. And Peter, they led by example. As a pediatrician, my dad helped lead kids and families through the stress of challenging, uncertain moments. And as an educator, my mom taught me and so many other kids to care about the world around us. Watching their work up close inspired me to follow in their footsteps — but in a different way. Instead of medicine or education, I wanted to help Pennsylvanians through public service. By cleaning up our government, solving problems, and making life better for the families I served. That’s what I did as a State Representative, when I helped pass some of the toughest ethics laws in Pennsylvania history. It’s what I did as Attorney General, when I got justice for survivors of sexual abuse, protected health care access, and won in court dozens of times to defend our democracy. And Peter, it’s what I’m doing right now as Pennsylvania’s 48th Governor. Together, we’ve cut taxes seven times, putting millions back into the pockets of parents, seniors, and small businesses. We’ve invested a record amount of money in public education and served over 90 million free breakfasts to Pennsylvania students. We’ve created over 18,000 jobs, grown our economy, and expanded opportunity across our Commonwealth. And yes, we’ve protected abortion rights, ensuring every Pennsylvanian has the freedom to access the health care they need. But my work isn’t finished. Today, Pennsylvanians are facing unprecedented challenges with a White House hell bent on raising costs, attacking our freedoms, and undermining our democracy. And this year, we have the chance to defeat the politics of chaos, division, and extremism that are making it harder across our Commonwealth. We have the opportunity to show the nation that hatred and division are not the way forward. That means standing up to the politicians who enable Donald Trump and JD Vance — even when they know their policies hurt Pennsylvanians. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately to Josh Shapiro and the Democratic Party of Georgia: | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you so much for your support. Let’s get to work. Josh Shapiro Governor of Pennsylvania | = Help Elect Georgia Democrats. name: Jon Ossoff for Senate
subject: Things are heating up in Georgia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter,
We try to do things differently around here. No phony 5x match emails. No overexaggerated claims that “Donald Trump is FINISHED!”
We never want to trick folks into donating and we certainly don’t expect this team to chip in to every email. _That’s just not our style._
We know these requests can be a lot so we try to give meaningful updates on the state of Jon Ossoff’s race and how this team can pitch in and help us win big in November.
So before we ask you to chip in today, let us explain why we’re asking for your help:
Things are really heating up in Georgia. _NBC News_ recently named Jon’s race as one of the most critical in the fight for the Senate. And, _The New York Times_ just labeled Georgia as a key race to watch in 2026.
This all comes at a time where one of Jon’s GOP opponents released an online ad attacking his record and spreading misinformation.
We need to respond and reach out to voters across the state, but that takes a massive amount of resources.
**[That’s why we’re hoping this team can help out again today. If you’re able — and only if you’re able — will you chip in $5 to Jon Ossoff’s campaign? We’ll put your donation right to work winning this battleground seat.](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-jan26.wPRyEn&refcode=em260117-1-f-a&t=1&akid=1458%2E5691347%2ERT2qqX)**

name: Team Sharice
subject: What happens in Kansas could determine the House majority
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
We wish we could say there was a silver lining

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123512285/595695699/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWE1OTU5OS1iY2YzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ReGX0FCMXOZ-I4tx7zc0A2NXR86UXNgQCOIuD4Ylvrs=) The last week has made it clearer than ever that what happens right here in Kansas could determine the House majority: **FIRST:** Donald Trump issued a decree to Republicans here in Kansas and all across the country, demanding an unprecedented mid-decade redistricting push to gerrymander Democrats like Sharice Davids out of office. **THEN:** The Kansas GOP obliged. They worked around the clock — even setting aside $460,000 in taxpayer funds to hold a special legislative session focused exclusively on gerrymandering. **NOW:** We’re in the middle of a _second_ redistricting battle... after the Kansas GOP’s plan to hold a special session fell flat on its face. We wish we could say there was a silver lining, and that Democrats are guaranteed to take back the House no matter what happens here in Kansas. But we can’t. _So we need to fight this gerrymander like the future of Kansas — and the future of our country — depends on it._ **Because it could.** **_[Please, can Sharice count on you to pitch in today? name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Looking to this November
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20260117-1832-foote&t=1001&akid=1832%2E342411%2EoT_j5w ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Trump and the MAGA crowd are terrified of Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and
Rashida.

They know the Squad is powerful together, and they’re scared of what they
represent: bold, progressive leadership that actually fights for working
people, communities of color, and everyone who’s been left behind.

The GOP will stop at nothing to silence our progressive champions, but the
Squad isn’t backing down. They’re standing up, speaking truth to power,
and fighting for our communities every single day — for economic justice,
racial justice, and real equity for all.

Here’s the reality: we have four seats to defend, four districts to
protect, and four communities that would be left vulnerable if MAGA wins.

Losing one seat wouldn’t just be a local loss, it would be a blow to
progress everywhere: healthcare, climate, workers’ rights, voting rights,
criminal justice. MAGA knows it. That’s why they’re coming for them.

We cannot and will not give them an inch, and we need you in this
fight, Peter.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260117-movement.b5WLNI&refcode=em20260117-1832&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1832%2E342411%2EoT_j5w ]Will you chip in $3 to the Squad Victory Fund today to
defend these four seats, and ensure our progressive champions have the
resources to win the fights ahead?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260117-movement.b5WLNI&refcode=em20260117-1832&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1832%2E342411%2EoT_j5w ]Contribute ››

Thank you,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1832%2E342411%2EoT_j5w

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Phil Weiser
subject: Hi there, I’m Phil
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Phil Weiser for Colorado
body:
Let me reintroduce myself.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123514013/595699448/1239926708?refcode=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ms=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2Q3ZjI5My1iZGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_2r_TM7pzmErVM9rENqcqgGnwC0wUTuZI4YtuRBMbas=&emci=bf98cc2f-23f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=51844520) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123514014/595699456/-246109695?refcode=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ms=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2Q3ZjI5My1iZGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_2r_TM7pzmErVM9rENqcqgGnwC0wUTuZI4YtuRBMbas=) Hi there, Phil here. **[Before I ask you to back our grassroots campaign](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123514018/595699462/-246109695?refcode=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ms=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2Q3ZjI5My1iZGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_2r_TM7pzmErVM9rENqcqgGnwC0wUTuZI4YtuRBMbas=)** , I want to reintroduce myself and explain why I hope to earn your support. I came to Colorado over 30 years ago for a job interview, and that decision changed my life forever. It was here that my wife, Heidi, and I raised our two wonderful kids — three, if you count our mostly-well-behaved dog, Zeke. I’ve dedicated my life to public service. And, as the son and grandson of Holocaust survivors, I’m committed to our shared American values of liberty, justice, and the idea that all people should live free from discrimination, hatred, and oppression. My first job after law school was here in Colorado, and it launched my career that included clerking for Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg, working for President Obama, and serving as the Dean of the University of Colorado Law School. From that first job to now, I have always believed that Colorado is the best place to live and the ideal place to raise a family. **I love our state, folks. And when you love something, you fight for it.** **And my commitment to fight for Colorado is why I don’t get it when others try to appease and "work with" bullies like the President and his cabinet.** **It does not work. It’s not what leaders do. Not now, not ever. We must take a stand, not be afraid, and not back down.** I know fighting for what’s right isn’t easy. Fights worth having rarely are. As the People’s Lawyer, I’ve had a lot of practice in these fights. It’s my job to enforce the rule of law, and no one is above it: not deceitful corporations, not abusive employers, and definitely not the federal government. In fact, as part of upholding the rule of law, I’ve taken the Trump administration to court 50 times (and counting) — and won a majority of the time. **I’m proud of the reputation I’ve built here in Colorado.** I’m not here to do “politics as usual.” I’m here to fight for you, not the powerful or wealthy few. And I am always going to show up, listen, and deliver for all of Colorado. **[I will not let billionaire money cloud our grassroots agenda or steer our focus away from affordability, opportunity, and defending our freedoms. That means I’m relying on committed supporters like you. I’d be honored if you’d donate $25 to this campaign today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123514021/595699474/-246109695?amount=25&refcode=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ms=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2Q3ZjI5My1iZGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_2r_TM7pzmErVM9rENqcqgGnwC0wUTuZI4YtuRBMbas=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $10 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123514025/595699487/-246109695?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ms=ea.4cd7f293-bdf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2Q3ZjI5My1iZGYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_2r_TM7pzmErVM9rENqcqgGnwC0wUTuZI4YtuRBMbas=)

name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: Trump’s retribution campaign is in full swing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260117-61088&t=1001&akid=61088%2E1407376%2ESMRH0M ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260117-61088&t=1002&akid=61088%2E1407376%2ESMRH0M ]Demand Justice PAC

Trump promised retribution if he was reelected. Now he’s delivering
through an ever-growing list of revenge prosecutions.

This is what happens when an authoritarian gets his hands back on the
levers of power.

First: Trump demanded the Department of Justice go after New York Attorney
General Letitia James — not because she broke the law, but because she had
the courage to hold him accountable for civil fraud.

Then: He turned his rage on former FBI Director James Comey, calling for
prosecution because Comey refused to bow to Trump’s lies and corruption.

Now: Trump has added another name to his list of so-called “political
enemies,” Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

Why? Because Powell wouldn’t rig interest rates to help Trump politically.
He followed the evidence. He did his job. And for that, Trump wants him
punished.

Trump is weaponizing the Department of Justice to do his dirty work,
turning it into a personal enforcement arm designed to intimidate, punish,
and silence anyone who stands in his way.

It’s authoritarianism in real time.

Demand Justice PAC is calling this out — loudly — because silence is
exactly what Trump is counting on. We’re sounding the alarm before this
abuse of power becomes normalized, and we’re asking if you’ll stand with
us today.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260117-1.OH5T4o&refcode=em20260117-61088&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=61088%2E1407376%2ESMRH0M ]If you’re outraged by Trump’s retribution campaign — if you refuse to
accept a justice system twisted into a weapon — chip in right now to
Demand Justice PAC.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260117-1.OH5T4o&amount=5&refcode=em20260117-61088&t=1022&akid=61088%2E1407376%2ESMRH0M ]Donate $5 ››

In solidarity,

clean_body: [ ]Demand Justice PAC[ ]Demand Justice PAC Trump promised retribution if he was reelected. Now he’s delivering through an ever-growing list of revenge prosecutions. This is what happens when an authoritarian gets his hands back on the levers of power. First: Trump demanded the Department of Justice go after New York Attorney General Letitia James — not because she broke the law, but because she had the courage to hold him accountable for civil fraud. Then: He turned his rage on former FBI Director James Comey, calling for prosecution because Comey refused to bow to Trump’s lies and corruption. Now: Trump has added another name to his list of so-called “political enemies,” Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Why? Because Powell wouldn’t rig interest rates to help Trump politically. He followed the evidence. He did his job. And for that, Trump wants him punished. Trump is weaponizing the Department of Justice to do his dirty work, turning it into a personal enforcement arm designed to intimidate, punish, and silence anyone who stands in his way. It’s authoritarianism in real time. Demand Justice PAC is calling this out — loudly — because silence is exactly what Trump is counting on. We’re sounding the alarm before this abuse of power becomes normalized, and we’re asking if you’ll stand with us today. [ ]If you’re outraged by Trump’s retribution campaign — if you refuse to accept a justice system twisted into a weapon — chip in right now to Demand Justice PAC. [ ]Donate $5 ›› In solidarity, Demand Justice PAC

Derek, What if we told you that by [**chipping in $7 today**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?refcode=em_20260117_5807_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5807_22930333_1T9Ydv&akid=5807%2E22930333%2E1T9Ydv), you could help solve one of the Democratic Party’s most pressing challenges? Look, over the last few years, Republicans have registered 2.4 million voters, while Democrats have suffered major losses in key states. So we’re doing something about it. This week, the DNC announced we will make partisan voter registration one of our top strategic priorities this year in the fight to win back the House and the Senate, and to help Democrats win down-ballot across the country in November. We’re launching “When We Count,” a first-of-its-kind initiative to reclaim responsibility for partisan voter registration and win back lost ground. If we’re successful, we might not only flip Congress in November, but also win critical down-ballot races and make headway toward taking back the White House in 2028. **So picture this: Today, your $7 donation helps us at the DNC register hundreds of thousands of new Democratic voters in key battleground states ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.** [**If ensuring we have the power of the next generation of Democratic voters behind us this November matters to you, we hope you’ll consider becoming a Founding Donor to support the DNC’s “When We Count” initiative today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?refcode=em_20260117_5807_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5807_22930333_1T9Ydv&akid=5807%2E22930333%2E1T9Ydv) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260117_5807_fr&refcode2=5807_22930333_1T9Ydv&akid=5807%2E22930333%2E1T9Ydv)**

clean_body: Derek, What if we told you that by , you could help solve one of the Democratic Party’s most pressing challenges? Look, over the last few years, Republicans have registered 2.4 million voters, while Democrats have suffered major losses in key states. So we’re doing something about it. This week, the DNC announced we will make partisan voter registration one of our top strategic priorities this year in the fight to win back the House and the Senate, and to help Democrats win down-ballot across the country in November. We’re launching “When We Count,” a first-of-its-kind initiative to reclaim responsibility for partisan voter registration and win back lost ground. If we’re successful, we might not only flip Congress in November, but also win critical down-ballot races and make headway toward taking back the White House in 2028. So picture this: Today, your $7 donation helps us at the DNC register hundreds of thousands of new Democratic voters in key battleground states ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you, The Democrats If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: Team Walz
subject: Fwd: Trump’s justice department is investigating me
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
#
Peter,
Last night the DOJ announced they’re investigating Governor Walz for demanding transparency and answers following the fatal ICE shooting in Minneapolis.
**[These fights are expensive by design. If you’re able, please chip in $5 today to help power our Legal Defense Fund and ensure we can keep standing up for accountability, transparency, and justice.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/235?refcode=tw-em-20260117-420&amount=5&t=1&akid=420%2E34582%2Eh4tF-m)**
Thank you,
Team Walz
* * *
Peter,
Two days ago it was Elissa Slotkin. Last week it was Jerome Powell. Before that, Mark Kelly.
Now, the federal government has opened an investigation into me.
This comes after a federal ICE agent fatally shot Renee Good in Minneapolis. But instead of focusing on accountability for that tragic shooting, federal authorities are turning their attention toward a governor who demanded transparency and answers.
The only person not being investigated for the shooting of Renee Good is the federal agent who shot her.
Weaponizing the justice system and threatening political opponents is a dangerous, authoritarian tactic. It undermines public trust and distracts from the real issue: a Minnesotan lost her life, and justice has yet to be served.
I won’t back down from defending Minnesotans or standing up for the rule of law. But these fights are expensive by design — meant to intimidate and drain resources so people who speak up are forced to stop.
**[If you’re able, please contribute $5 to our Legal Defense Fund today and help ensure we can keep fighting for accountability, transparency, and justice.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/235?refcode=tw-em-20260117-420&amount=5&t=2&akid=420%2E34582%2Eh4tF-m)**
Thank you for standing with me in this moment.
I’m grateful,
Tim Walz

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dgalegalfund?refcode=tw-em-20260117-420&t=3&refcode2=420_34582_h4tF-m&akid=420%2E34582%2Eh4tF-m)**

| | We can’t let this go under the radar: the U.S. Supreme Court may be on the verge of decimating fair representation. If, as seems likely, the far-right majority guts Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act in _Louisiana v. Callais_ , lawmakers will be able to leap into a frenzy of racially discriminatory redistricting. **And this could happen any day now. ** The new maps lawmakers could draw could dramatically dilute Black and Brown Americans’ political power — moving people of color into districts where their voices are left out and their freedom to elect the candidates of their choice is stripped. Our analysis with Black Voters Matter Fund identified that such a ruling could secure Republicans 19 more safe U.S. House seats — significantly undercutting Black and Latino representation in Congress and cementing long-term one-party control even if Republicans lose the overall popular vote. And our latest report laid bare how GOP legislatures could eliminate approximately 191 state legislative seats currently held by Democrats — the vast majority of which are held by Black representatives in majority-minority districts. **We’ll still have a chance to fight back for fair maps. But first, we need to make sure as many people as possible know about the threat we face. That way, they’ll be prepared to respond.** **So there’s a concrete action we’re asking you to take today: [share our new report that outlines the potential ruling’s sweeping consequences.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123515679/595704646/325450178?refcode=emm_ffa_20260117_T1_ShareVRA2.0Report_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMDZkZTA0MC1iZWYzLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDBkM2E1NGI1NjAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Wvh7nOAjUpcR2VtnTwvv_attYxs_s00UEo6vUpbIOUs=&emci=276de040-bef3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=306de040-bef3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&ceid=1037277)**

subject: help us stay afloat?

name: Roy Cooper HQ
subject: 2️⃣ reasons this race needs your attention, Peter
body:
This is why we need your help.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
| **[<<< Donate $2 to Roy Cooper’s U.S. Senate Campaign >>>](https://act.roycooper.com/go/4374?amount=2&t=7&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)**
---
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/4374?t=8&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
_We’re asking for $2 today, Peter. Here are 2 reasons why:_
→ Democrats have the advantage going into 2026. Voters are getting fed up with the GOP’s corruption & chaos. **We cannot afford to get complacent now.**
→ Any path to retaking the Senate majority runs through North Carolina. We have to win here — but our race is rated a TOSS-UP, and our victory isn’t a guarantee.
**[Stopping the GOP starts in North Carolina. If we can win here, we can turn the tide. Donate $2 to send Roy to the U.S. Senate to fight for us.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/4374?amount=2&t=9&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)**
Thanks,
Team Cooper
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?source=&t=4&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=10&akid=352%2E124072%2EGUM2u8)
